I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
[feature] Add STS AssumeRole (cross-account IAM) support for Kafka async consumer
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently, Centrifugo's Kafka async consumer supports AWS MSK IAM ("aws-msk-iam" SASL mechanism) by configuring AccessKey/SecretKey via the SASL user/password fields. However, it is missing support for using [AWS STS AssumeRole](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_AssumeRole.html) for IAM authentication. This is needed to easily and securely connect to MSK clusters hosted in *other* AWS accounts, or when following best practices that require only temporary credentials.

Many organizations use cross-account setups (for instance, running Centrifugo in one AWS account and hosting MSK in another) and would prefer to use role assumption instead of sharing or rotating long-term IAM keys. This would also make it consistent with the SQS consumer, which already supports an `assume_role_arn` config field for cross-account access.

**Describe the solution you'd like.**

- Add `assume_role_arn` (or similar) and, optionally, `credentials_profile` options to the Kafka consumer config, matching the SQS consumer configuration style.
- At runtime, if the option is set, Centrifugo should use AWS STS (via the SDK) to obtain temporary credentials and use those for SASL authentication.
- The configuration should continue to support direct key input for backwards compatibility, but if both are set, `assume_role_arn` should take precedence.
- Document this option in the Kafka consumer section of the docs, including an example cross-account scenario.

**Who will benefit from this feature?**

- Teams needing to securely connect Centrifugo to MSK in other AWS accounts
- Users following AWS best practices (no long-lived IAM users/keys)
- Environments requiring tighter IAM and audit controls

**Additional context**
- SQS consumer already supports assume role via `assume_role_arn`. Parity in Kafka consumer would be very useful.
- This feature would use the AWS Go SDK's `stscreds` provider under the hood, as with SQS.

</pr_description>

    16	        "github.com/centrifugal/centrifugo/v6/internal/logging"
    17	        "github.com/centrifugal/centrifugo/v6/internal/metrics"
    18	
    19	        "github.com/rs/zerolog"
    20	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/kgo"
    21	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/kmsg"
    22	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/aws"
    23	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/plain"
    24	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/scram"
    25	)
    26	
    27	type KafkaConfig = configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig
    28	
    29	type testOnlyConfig struct {
    30	        topicPartitionBeforePauseCh    chan topicPartition
    31	        topicPartitionPauseProceedCh   chan struct{}
    32	        fetchTopicPartitionSubmittedCh chan kgo.FetchTopicPartition
    33	        // leaveGroupOnShutdown when true sends a manual LeaveGroup request on shutdown.
    34	        // Used only in tests to simulate the old behavior with dynamic instance IDs.
    35	        leaveGroupOnShutdown bool
    36	}
    37	
    38	type topicPartition struct {
    39	        topic     string
    40	        partition int32
    41	}
    42	
    43	type KafkaConsumer struct {
    44	        name       string
    45	        client     *kgo.Client
    46	        dispatcher Dispatcher
    47	        config     KafkaConfig
    48	        // consumersMu protects the consumers map. During normal operation, concurrent access is already
    49	        // prevented: franz-go serializes assigned/revoked/lost callbacks, and BlockRebalanceOnPoll
    50	        // prevents callbacks from firing while pollUntilFatal reads the map. The mutex is needed
    51	        // for shutdown, when the main goroutine accesses the map (via allTopicPartitions/killConsumers)
    52	        // while the group management goroutine may still be running callbacks. Must not be held
    53	        // during killConsumers' wg.Wait() to avoid blocking concurrent callbacks.
    54	        consumersMu    sync.Mutex
    55	        consumers      map[topicPartition]*partitionConsumer
    56	        doneCh         chan struct{}
    57	        common         *consumerCommon
    58	        testOnlyConfig testOnlyConfig
    59	}
    60	
    61	func NewKafkaConsumer(
    62	        config KafkaConfig, dispatcher Dispatcher, common *consumerCommon,
    63	) (*KafkaConsumer, error) {
    64	        if len(config.Brokers) == 0 {
    65	                return nil, errors.New("brokers required")
    66	        }
    67	        if len(config.Topics) == 0 {
    68	                return nil, errors.New("topics required")
    69	        }
    70	        if len(config.ConsumerGroup) == 0 {
    71	                return nil, errors.New("consumer_group required")
    72	        }
    73	        if config.MaxPollRecords == 0 {
    74	                config.MaxPollRecords = 100
    75	        }
    76	        if config.FetchMaxWait == 0 {
    77	                config.FetchMaxWait = configtypes.Duration(500 * time.Millisecond)
    78	        }
    79	        if config.PartitionQueueMaxSize == -1 {
    80	                config.PartitionQueueMaxSize = 0
    81	        } else if config.PartitionQueueMaxSize == 0 {
    82	                config.PartitionQueueMaxSize = 1000
    83	        }
    84	        consumer := &KafkaConsumer{
    85	                name:       common.name,
    86	                dispatcher: dispatcher,
    87	                config:     config,
    88	                consumers:  make(map[topicPartition]*partitionConsumer),
    89	                doneCh:     make(chan struct{}),
    90	                common:     common,
    91	        }
    92	        cl, err := consumer.initClient()
    93	        if err != nil {
    94	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error init Kafka client: %w", err)
    95	        }
    96	        consumer.client = cl
    97	        return consumer, nil
    98	}
    99	
   100	const kafkaClientID = "centrifugo"
   101	
   102	// kgoZerologAdapter adapts zerolog.Logger to kgo.Logger interface.
   103	type kgoZerologAdapter struct {
   104	        log zerolog.Logger
   105	}
   106	
   107	func (l *kgoZerologAdapter) Level() kgo.LogLevel {
   108	        switch {
   109	        case logging.Enabled(logging.TraceLevel):
   110	                return kgo.LogLevelDebug // Kafka client debug level is more like our trace.
   111	        case logging.Enabled(logging.InfoLevel):
   112	                return kgo.LogLevelInfo
   113	        case logging.Enabled(logging.WarnLevel):
   114	                return kgo.LogLevelWarn
   115	        case logging.Enabled(logging.ErrorLevel):
   116	                return kgo.LogLevelError
   117	        default:
   118	                return kgo.LogLevelNone
   119	        }
   120	}
   121	
   122	func (l *kgoZerologAdapter) Log(level kgo.LogLevel, msg string, keyvals ...any) {
   123	        var e *zerolog.Event
   124	        switch level {
   125	        case kgo.LogLevelError:
   126	                e = l.log.Error()
   127	        case kgo.LogLevelWarn:
   128	                e = l.log.Warn()
   129	        case kgo.LogLevelInfo:
   130	                e = l.log.Info()
   131	        case kgo.LogLevelDebug:
   132	                e = l.log.Trace() // Kafka client debug level is more like our trace.
   133	        default:
   134	                return
   135	        }
   136	        for i := 0; i+1 < len(keyvals); i += 2 {
   137	                if key, ok := keyvals[i].(string); ok {
   138	                        e = e.Interface(key, keyvals[i+1])
   139	                }
   140	        }
   141	        e.Msg(msg)
   142	}
   143	
   144	// leaveGroup sends a manual LeaveGroup request for static members. franz-go's Close does not
   145	// send LeaveGroup for static members by design. This method is only used in tests to simulate
   146	// the old behavior where dynamic instance IDs were combined with a manual LeaveGroup.
   147	func (c *KafkaConsumer) leaveGroup(ctx context.Context, client *kgo.Client) error {
   148	        req := kmsg.NewPtrLeaveGroupRequest()
   149	        req.Group = c.config.ConsumerGroup
   150	        instanceID := c.config.InstanceID
   151	        reason := "shutdown"
   152	        req.Members = []kmsg.LeaveGroupRequestMember{
   153	                {
   154	                        InstanceID: &instanceID,
   155	                        Reason:     &reason,
   156	                },
   157	        }
   158	        _, err := req.RequestWith(ctx, client)
   159	        return err
   160	}
   161	
   162	func (c *KafkaConsumer) initClient() (*kgo.Client, error) {
   163	        opts := []kgo.Opt{
   164	                kgo.SeedBrokers(c.config.Brokers...),
   165	                kgo.ConsumeTopics(c.config.Topics...),
   166	                kgo.ConsumerGroup(c.config.ConsumerGroup),
   167	                kgo.OnPartitionsAssigned(c.assigned),
   168	                kgo.OnPartitionsRevoked(c.revoked),
   169	                kgo.OnPartitionsLost(c.lost),
   170	                kgo.AutoCommitMarks(),
   171	                kgo.BlockRebalanceOnPoll(),
   172	                kgo.ClientID(kafkaClientID),
   173	                kgo.FetchMaxWait(time.Duration(c.config.FetchMaxWait)),
   174	                kgo.WithLogger(&kgoZerologAdapter{log: c.common.log}),
   175	        }
   176	        if c.config.InstanceID != "" { // Important to keep separate, otherwise pointer to empty string is used in kgo.InstanceID.
   177	                opts = append(opts, kgo.InstanceID(c.config.InstanceID))
   178	        }
   179	        if c.config.FetchMaxBytes > 0 {
   180	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchMaxBytes(c.config.FetchMaxBytes))
   181	        }
   182	        if !c.config.FetchReadUncommitted {
   183	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadCommitted()))
   184	        } else {
   185	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadUncommitted()))
   186	        }
   187	        if c.config.TLS.Enabled {
   188	                tlsConfig, err := c.config.TLS.ToGoTLSConfig("kafka:" + c.name)
   189	                if err != nil {
   190	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("error making TLS configuration: %w", err)
   191	                }
   192	                dialer := &tls.Dialer{
   193	                        NetDialer: &net.Dialer{Timeout: 10 * time.Second},
   194	                        Config:    tlsConfig,
   195	                }
   196	                opts = append(opts, kgo.Dialer(dialer.DialContext))
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        if c.config.SASLMechanism != "" {
   200	                switch c.config.SASLMechanism {
   201	                case "plain":
   202	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(plain.Auth{
   203	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   204	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   205	                        }.AsMechanism()))
   206	                case "scram-sha-256":
   207	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   208	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   209	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   210	                        }.AsSha256Mechanism()))
   211	                case "scram-sha-512":
   212	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   213	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   214	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   215	                        }.AsSha512Mechanism()))
   216	                case "aws-msk-iam":
   217	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(aws.Auth{
   218	                                AccessKey: c.config.SASLUser,
   219	                                SecretKey: c.config.SASLPassword,
   220	                        }.AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()))
   221	                default:
   222	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported SASL mechanism: %s", c.config.SASLMechanism)
   223	                }
   224	        }
   225	
   226	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(opts...)
   227	        if err != nil {
   228	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error initializing client: %w", err)
   229	        }
   230	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   231	        defer cancel()
   232	        if err := client.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
   233	                client.Close()
   234	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error ping Kafka: %w", err)
   235	        }
   236	        return client, nil
   237	}
   238	
   239	func (c *KafkaConsumer) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   240	        defer func() {
   241	                close(c.doneCh)
   242	                if c.client != nil {
   243	                        // Stop all partition consumers first to ensure all offsets are marked.
   244	                        c.killConsumers(c.allTopicPartitions())
   245	                        if c.testOnlyConfig.leaveGroupOnShutdown && c.config.InstanceID != "" {
   246	                                // Test-only path simulating old behavior with dynamic instance IDs.
   247	                                // Commit before manual LeaveGroup — the coordinator rejects commits
   248	                                // from members that already left the group.
   249	                                closeCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   250	                                defer cancel()
   251	                                if err := c.client.CommitMarkedOffsets(closeCtx); err != nil {
   252	                                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error committing marked offsets on shutdown")
   253	                                }
   254	                                if err := c.leaveGroup(closeCtx, c.client); err != nil {
   255	                                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error leaving consumer group")
   256	                                }
   257	                        }
   258	                        // CloseAllowingRebalance calls AllowRebalance (required since we use
   259	                        // BlockRebalanceOnPoll) and then Close. Close triggers the manage loop
   260	                        // exit which calls onRevoked with a valid client context and no
   261	                        // joinAndSync WLock contention — so CommitMarkedOffsets in onRevoked
   262	                        // succeeds without racing. For non-static members (no InstanceID),
   263	                        // Close sends LeaveGroup automatically. For static members (InstanceID
   264	                        // is set), Close does not send LeaveGroup – so the replacement consumer
   265	                        // with the same instance ID can take over partitions seamlessly.
   266	                        c.client.CloseAllowingRebalance()
   267	                }
   268	        }()
   269	        for {
   270	                err := c.pollUntilFatal(ctx)
   271	                if err != nil {
   272	                        if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   273	                                return ctx.Err()
   274	                        }
   275	                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error polling Kafka")
   276	                }
   277	                // Upon returning from polling loop we are re-initializing consumer client.
   278	                c.client.CloseAllowingRebalance()
   279	                c.client = nil
   280	                c.common.log.Info().Msg("re-initializing Kafka consumer client")
   281	                err = c.reInitClient(ctx)
   282	                if err != nil {
   283	                        // Only context.Canceled may be returned.
   284	                        return err
   285	                }
   286	                c.common.log.Info().Msg("Kafka consumer client re-initialized")
   287	        }
   288	}
   289	
   290	func (c *KafkaConsumer) pollUntilFatal(ctx context.Context) error {
   291	        for {
   292	                select {
   293	                case <-ctx.Done():
   294	                        return ctx.Err()
   295	                default:
   296	                        fetches := c.client.PollRecords(ctx, c.config.MaxPollRecords)
   297	                        if fetches.IsClientClosed() {
   298	                                return nil
   299	                        }
   300	                        fetchErrors := fetches.Errors()
   301	                        if len(fetchErrors) > 0 {
   302	                                // Non-retryable errors returned. We will restart consumer client, but log errors first.
   303	                                var errs []error
   304	                                for _, fetchErr := range fetchErrors {
   305	                                        if errors.Is(fetchErr.Err, context.Canceled) {
   306	                                                return ctx.Err()
   307	                                        }
   308	                                        errs = append(errs, fetchErr.Err)
   309	                                        c.common.log.Error().Err(fetchErr.Err).Str("topic", fetchErr.Topic).Int32("partition", fetchErr.Partition).Msg("error while polling Kafka")
   310	                                }
   311	                                return fmt.Errorf("poll error: %w", errors.Join(errs...))
   312	                        }
   313	
   314	                        // Track which partitions we've paused in this poll cycle.
   315	                        pausedTopicPartitions := map[topicPartition]struct{}{}
   316	
   317	                        fetches.EachPartition(func(p kgo.FetchTopicPartition) {
   318	                                if len(p.Records) == 0 {
   319	                                        return
   320	                                }
   321	
   322	                                tp := topicPartition{p.Topic, p.Partition}
   323	
   324	                                consumer := c.consumers[tp]
   325	                                if consumer == nil {
   326	                                        return
   327	                                }
   328	
   329	                                select {
   330	                                case <-ctx.Done():
   331	                                        return
   332	                                case <-consumer.partitionCtx.Done():
   333	                                        return
   334	                                default:
   335	                                }
   336	
   337	                                partitionsToPause := map[string][]int32{p.Topic: {p.Partition}}
   338	                                if _, paused := pausedTopicPartitions[tp]; !paused {
   339	                                        // Only pause if queue threshold would be exceeded after adding records, or if threshold is 0 (always pause).
   340	                                        shouldPause := consumer.queue.NumRecords()+len(p.Records) >= c.config.PartitionQueueMaxSize
   341	                                        if shouldPause {
   342	                                                // Pause partition BEFORE submitting to queue to avoid race condition
   343	                                                // between pause and resume operations.
   344	                                                if c.testOnlyConfig.topicPartitionBeforePauseCh != nil {
   345	                                                        c.testOnlyConfig.topicPartitionBeforePauseCh <- tp
   346	                                                }
   347	                                                if c.testOnlyConfig.topicPartitionPauseProceedCh != nil {
   348	                                                        <-c.testOnlyConfig.topicPartitionPauseProceedCh
   349	                                                }
   350	                                                c.client.PauseFetchPartitions(partitionsToPause)
   351	                                                pausedTopicPartitions[tp] = struct{}{}
   352	                                        }
   353	                                }
   354	
   355	                                // Now submit to unbounded queue.
   356	                                if !consumer.queue.Push(p) {
   357	                                        // Queue is closed, partition consumer is shutting down
   358	                                        // Resume the partition if we paused it but won't process
   359	                                        if _, wasPaused := pausedTopicPartitions[tp]; wasPaused {
   360	                                                c.client.ResumeFetchPartitions(partitionsToPause)
   361	                                        }
   362	                                        return
   363	                                }
   364	
   365	                                if c.testOnlyConfig.fetchTopicPartitionSubmittedCh != nil {
   366	                                        c.testOnlyConfig.fetchTopicPartitionSubmittedCh <- p
   367	                                }
   368	                        })
   369	
   370	                        c.client.AllowRebalance()
   371	                }
   372	        }
   373	}
   374	
   375	func (c *KafkaConsumer) reInitClient(ctx context.Context) error {
   376	        var backoffDuration time.Duration = 0
   377	        retries := 0
   378	        for {
   379	                select {
   380	                case <-ctx.Done():
   381	                        return ctx.Err()
   382	                default:
   383	                }
   384	                client, err := c.initClient()
   385	                if err != nil {
   386	                        retries++
   387	                        backoffDuration = getNextBackoffDuration(backoffDuration, retries)
   388	                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error initializing Kafka client")
   389	                        select {
   390	                        case <-ctx.Done():
   391	                                return ctx.Err()
   392	                        case <-time.After(backoffDuration):
   393	                                continue
   394	                        }
   395	                }
   396	                c.client = client
   397	                break
   398	        }
   399	        return nil
   400	}
   401	
   402	func (c *KafkaConsumer) assigned(ctx context.Context, cl *kgo.Client, assigned map[string][]int32) {
   403	        c.consumersMu.Lock()
   404	        defer c.consumersMu.Unlock()
   405	        select {
   406	        case <-c.doneCh:
   407	                return // Shutdown in progress, skip creating consumers that will be immediately killed.
   408	        default:
   409	        }
   410	        for topic, partitions := range assigned {
   411	                for _, partition := range partitions {
   412	                        partitionCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
   413	                        pc := &partitionConsumer{
   414	                                partitionCtx: partitionCtx,
   415	                                cancelFunc:   cancel,
   416	                                dispatcher:   c.dispatcher,
   417	                                cl:           cl,
   418	                                topic:        topic,
   419	                                partition:    partition,
   420	                                config:       c.config,
   421	                                name:         c.name,
   422	                                common:       c.common,
   423	
   424	                                done:  make(chan struct{}),
   425	                                queue: newUnboundedQueue(),
   426	                        }
   427	                        c.consumers[topicPartition{topic, partition}] = pc
   428	                        go pc.consume()
   429	                }
   430	        }
   431	}
   432	
   433	func (c *KafkaConsumer) revoked(ctx context.Context, cl *kgo.Client, revoked map[string][]int32) {
   434	        c.killConsumers(revoked)
   435	        // Always commit marked offsets on revoke.
   436	        if err := cl.CommitMarkedOffsets(ctx); err != nil {
   437	                c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error committing marked offsets on revoke")
   438	        }
   439	}
   440	
   441	func (c *KafkaConsumer) lost(_ context.Context, _ *kgo.Client, lost map[string][]int32) {
   442	        c.killConsumers(lost)
   443	        // Losing means we cannot commit: an error happened.
   444	}
   445	
   446	func (c *KafkaConsumer) allTopicPartitions() map[string][]int32 {
   447	        c.consumersMu.Lock()
   448	        defer c.consumersMu.Unlock()
   449	        result := make(map[string][]int32)
   450	        for tp := range c.consumers {
   451	                result[tp.topic] = append(result[tp.topic], tp.partition)
   452	        }
   453	        return result
   454	}
   455	
   456	func (c *KafkaConsumer) killConsumers(lost map[string][]int32) {
   457	        c.consumersMu.Lock()
   458	        var pcs []*partitionConsumer
   459	        for topic, partitions := range lost {
   460	                for _, partition := range partitions {
   461	                        tp := topicPartition{topic, partition}
   462	                        pc := c.consumers[tp]
   463	                        if pc == nil {
   464	                                continue
   465	                        }
   466	                        delete(c.consumers, tp)
   467	                        pcs = append(pcs, pc)
   468	                }
   469	        }
   470	        c.consumersMu.Unlock()
   471	
   472	        var wg sync.WaitGroup
   473	        for _, pc := range pcs {
   474	                pc.queue.Close()
   475	                pc.cancelFunc()
   476	                wg.Add(1)
   477	                go func() { <-pc.done; wg.Done() }()
   478	        }
   479	        wg.Wait()
   480	}
   481	
   482	type partitionConsumer struct {
   483	        partitionCtx context.Context
   484	        cancelFunc   context.CancelFunc
   485	        dispatcher   Dispatcher
   486	        cl           *kgo.Client
   487	        topic        string
   488	        partition    int32
   489	        config       KafkaConfig
   490	        name         string
   491	        common       *consumerCommon
   492	
   493	        done  chan struct{}
   494	        queue *unboundedQueue
   495	}
   496	
   497	func getUint64HeaderValue(record *kgo.Record, headerKey string) (uint64, error) {
   498	        if headerKey == "" {
   499	                return 0, nil
   500	        }
   501	        headerValue := getHeaderValue(record, headerKey)
   502	        if headerValue == "" {
   503	                return 0, nil
   504	        }
   505	        value, err := strconv.ParseUint(headerValue, 10, 64)
   506	        if err != nil {
   507	                return 0, fmt.Errorf("error parsing header %s value %s: %w", headerKey, headerValue, err)
   508	        }
   509	        return value, nil
   510	}
   511	
   512	func getBoolHeaderValue(record *kgo.Record, headerKey string) (bool, error) {
   513	        if headerKey == "" {
   514	                return false, nil
   515	        }
   516	        headerValue := getHeaderValue(record, headerKey)
   517	        if headerValue == "" {
   518	                return false, nil
   519	        }
   520	        value, err := strconv.ParseBool(headerValue)
   521	        if err != nil {
   522	                return false, fmt.Errorf("error parsing header %s value %s: %w", headerKey, headerValue, err)
   523	        }
   524	        return value, nil
   525	}
   526	
   527	func getHeaderValue(record *kgo.Record, headerKey string) string {
   528	        if headerKey == "" {
   529	                return ""
   530	        }
   531	        for _, header := range record.Headers {
   532	                if header.Key == headerKey {

    28	type AwsSqsConsumerConfig = configtypes.AwsSqsConsumerConfig
    29	
    30	// AwsSqsConsumer consumes messages from AWS SQS (or SNS delivered to SQS).
    31	// It uses per-group ordering when enabled and supports both command and publication data modes.
    32	type AwsSqsConsumer struct {
    33	        config     AwsSqsConsumerConfig
    34	        dispatcher Dispatcher
    35	        client     *sqs.Client
    36	        common     *consumerCommon
    37	}
    38	
    39	type overrideEndpointResolver struct {
    40	        Endpoint endpoints.Endpoint
    41	}
    42	
    43	func (o overrideEndpointResolver) ResolveEndpoint(_ context.Context, _ sqs.EndpointParameters) (endpoints.Endpoint, error) {
    44	        return o.Endpoint, nil
    45	}
    46	
    47	// NewAwsSqsConsumer creates and initializes a new AwsSqsConsumer.
    48	func NewAwsSqsConsumer(
    49	        cfg AwsSqsConsumerConfig, dispatcher Dispatcher, common *consumerCommon,
    50	) (*AwsSqsConsumer, error) {
    51	        if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
    52	                return nil, err
    53	        }
    54	
    55	        loadOpts := []func(*config.LoadOptions) error{
    56	                config.WithRegion(cfg.Region),
    57	        }
    58	        if cfg.CredentialsProfile != "" {
    59	                loadOpts = append(loadOpts, config.WithSharedConfigProfile(cfg.CredentialsProfile))
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    63	        defer cancel()
    64	        awsCfg, err := config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, loadOpts...)
    65	        if err != nil {
    66	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config: %w", err)
    67	        }
    68	
    69	        var sqsOpts []func(*sqs.Options)
    70	        var client *sqs.Client
    71	        if cfg.LocalStackEndpoint != "" {
    72	                parsedURL, err := url.Parse(cfg.LocalStackEndpoint)
    73	                if err != nil {
    74	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse LocalStack endpoint: %w", err)
    75	                }
    76	                sqsOpts = append(sqsOpts,
    77	                        sqs.WithEndpointResolverV2(overrideEndpointResolver{
    78	                                Endpoint: endpoints.Endpoint{
    79	                                        URI: *parsedURL,
    80	                                },
    81	                        }),
    82	                )
    83	        }
    84	
    85	        // If AssumeRoleARN is provided, assume that role.
    86	        if cfg.AssumeRoleARN != "" {
    87	                stsClient := sts.NewFromConfig(awsCfg)
    88	                assumeRoleProvider := stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider(stsClient, cfg.AssumeRoleARN)
    89	                awsCfg.Credentials = aws.NewCredentialsCache(assumeRoleProvider)
    90	        }
    91	
    92	        client = sqs.NewFromConfig(awsCfg, sqsOpts...)
    93	
    94	        consumer := &AwsSqsConsumer{
    95	                config:     cfg,
    96	                dispatcher: dispatcher,
    97	                client:     client,
    98	                common:     common,
    99	        }
   100	        return consumer, nil
   101	}
   102	
   103	// Run is the main loop that receives messages from each queue and processes them in batches.
   104	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   105	        var wg sync.WaitGroup
   106	
   107	        maxConcurrency := c.config.MaxConcurrency
   108	        if maxConcurrency < 1 {
   109	                maxConcurrency = 1
   110	        }
   111	
   112	        // Spawn one goroutine per queue URL.
   113	        for _, queueURL := range c.config.Queues {
   114	                wg.Add(1)
   115	                go func(qURL string) {
   116	                        defer wg.Done()
   117	                        for {
   118	                                select {
   119	                                case <-ctx.Done():
   120	                                        return
   121	                                default:
   122	                                }
   123	
   124	                                waitTimeSeconds := int32(c.config.PollWaitTime.ToDuration().Seconds())
   125	                                if waitTimeSeconds < 1 {
   126	                                        waitTimeSeconds = 1
   127	                                }
   128	
   129	                                visibilityTimeoutSeconds := int32(c.config.VisibilityTimeout.ToDuration().Seconds())
   130	                                if visibilityTimeoutSeconds < 1 {
   131	                                        visibilityTimeoutSeconds = 1
   132	                                }
   133	
   134	                                out, err := c.client.ReceiveMessage(ctx, &sqs.ReceiveMessageInput{
   135	                                        QueueUrl:                    aws.String(qURL),
   136	                                        MaxNumberOfMessages:         c.config.MaxNumberOfMessages,
   137	                                        WaitTimeSeconds:             waitTimeSeconds,
   138	                                        VisibilityTimeout:           visibilityTimeoutSeconds,
   139	                                        MessageAttributeNames:       []string{"All"},
   140	                                        MessageSystemAttributeNames: []types.MessageSystemAttributeName{"All"},
   141	                                })
   142	                                if err != nil {
   143	                                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msgf("failed to receive messages from queue %s", qURL)
   144	                                        select {
   145	                                        case <-ctx.Done():
   146	                                                return
   147	                                        case <-time.After(1 * time.Second):
   148	                                        }
   149	                                        continue
   150	                                }
   151	
   152	                                if logging.Enabled(logging.DebugLevel) {
   153	                                        c.common.log.Debug().Str("queue", qURL).Int("num_messages", len(out.Messages)).
   154	                                                Msg("received messages from queue")
   155	                                }
   156	
   157	                                if len(out.Messages) == 0 {
   158	                                        continue
   159	                                }
   160	
   161	                                c.processMessages(ctx, out.Messages, qURL, maxConcurrency)
   162	                        }
   163	                }(queueURL)
   164	        }
   165	
   166	        // Wait for context cancellation.
   167	        <-ctx.Done()
   168	        wg.Wait()
   169	        return ctx.Err()
   170	}
   171	
   172	// processMessages partitions messages into unordered and ordered groups,
   173	// then processes them appropriately. It passes the queue URL to batch deletion.
   174	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) processMessages(
   175	        ctx context.Context, messages []types.Message, queueURL string, maxConcurrency int,
   176	) {
   177	        // Partition messages into unordered and ordered groups.
   178	        var unorderedMessages []types.Message
   179	        orderedGroups := make(map[string][]types.Message, len(messages))
   180	        for _, msg := range messages {
   181	                groupID := ""
   182	                if msg.Attributes != nil {
   183	                        if id, ok := msg.Attributes[string(types.MessageSystemAttributeNameMessageGroupId)]; ok && id != "" {
   184	                                groupID = id
   185	                        }
   186	                }
   187	                if groupID == "" {
   188	                        unorderedMessages = append(unorderedMessages, msg)
   189	                } else {
   190	                        orderedGroups[groupID] = append(orderedGroups[groupID], msg)
   191	                }
   192	        }
   193	
   194	        // Channels or maps to collect successfully processed messages.
   195	        var (
   196	                unorderedProcessed []types.Message
   197	                orderedProcessed   = make(map[string][]types.Message)
   198	        )
   199	
   200	        var wg sync.WaitGroup
   201	        var mu sync.Mutex
   202	
   203	        sem := make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrency)
   204	
   205	        // Process unordered messages concurrently.
   206	        for _, msg := range unorderedMessages {
   207	                wg.Add(1)
   208	                sem <- struct{}{}
   209	                go func(m types.Message) {
   210	                        defer wg.Done()
   211	                        defer func() { <-sem }()
   212	                        if c.processSingleMessage(ctx, m) {
   213	                                mu.Lock()
   214	                                unorderedProcessed = append(unorderedProcessed, m)
   215	                                mu.Unlock()
   216	                        }
   217	                }(msg)
   218	        }
   219	
   220	        // Process each ordered group sequentially in its own goroutine.
   221	        for groupID, groupMessages := range orderedGroups {
   222	                wg.Add(1)
   223	                sem <- struct{}{}
   224	                go func(groupID string, groupMessages []types.Message) {
   225	                        defer wg.Done()
   226	                        defer func() { <-sem }()
   227	                        processedGroup := make([]types.Message, 0, len(groupMessages))
   228	                        // Process messages in the group sequentially.
   229	                        for _, m := range groupMessages {
   230	                                if c.processSingleMessage(ctx, m) {
   231	                                        processedGroup = append(processedGroup, m)
   232	                                } else {
   233	                                        // Abort further processing in this group if one fails.
   234	                                        break
   235	                                }
   236	                        }
   237	                        if len(processedGroup) > 0 {
   238	                                mu.Lock()
   239	                                orderedProcessed[groupID] = processedGroup
   240	                                mu.Unlock()
   241	                        }
   242	                }(groupID, groupMessages)
   243	        }
   244	
   245	        wg.Wait()
   246	
   247	        allProcessed := unorderedProcessed
   248	        for _, groupProcessed := range orderedProcessed {
   249	                allProcessed = append(allProcessed, groupProcessed...)
   250	        }
   251	        if len(allProcessed) > 0 {
   252	                c.batchDeleteMessages(ctx, allProcessed, queueURL)
   253	        }
   254	}
   255	
   256	// processSingleMessage handles processing of a single message with a maximum number of retries.
   257	// It returns true if the message is processed successfully (so it can be deleted).
   258	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) processSingleMessage(ctx context.Context, msg types.Message) bool {
   259	        data, err := c.extractMessageData(&msg)
   260	        if err != nil {
   261	                c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("failed to extract message data")
   262	                // Even on extraction errors, delete the message to prevent reprocessing.
   263	                return true
   264	        }
   265	
   266	        var retries int
   267	        var backoffDuration time.Duration
   268	        maxRetries := 3
   269	
   270	        for {
   271	                var processErr error
   272	                if c.config.PublicationDataMode.Enabled {
   273	                        processErr = c.processPublicationDataMessage(ctx, msg, data)
   274	                } else {
   275	                        processErr = c.processCommandMessage(ctx, msg, data)
   276	                }
   277	                if processErr == nil {
   278	                        if retries > 0 {
   279	                                c.common.log.Info().Msg("message processed successfully after retries")
   280	                        }
   281	                        break
   282	                }
   283	                if errors.Is(processErr, context.Canceled) {
   284	                        return false
   285	                }
   286	                retries++
   287	                if retries > maxRetries {
   288	                        if logging.Enabled(logging.DebugLevel) {
   289	                                c.common.log.Debug().Msg("max retries reached for processing message")
   290	                        }
   291	                        return false
   292	                }
   293	                metrics.ConsumerErrorsTotal.WithLabelValues(c.common.name).Inc()
   294	                c.common.log.Error().Err(processErr).Msg("error processing message, retrying")
   295	                backoffDuration = getNextBackoffDuration(backoffDuration, retries)
   296	                select {
   297	                case <-time.After(backoffDuration):
   298	                case <-ctx.Done():
   299	                        return false
   300	                }
   301	        }
   302	        metrics.ConsumerProcessedTotal.WithLabelValues(c.common.name).Inc()
   303	        return true
   304	}
   305	
   306	// batchDeleteMessages issues DeleteMessageBatch requests in batches of up to 10 messages.
   307	// It logs failures from each batch deletion call.
   308	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) batchDeleteMessages(ctx context.Context, messages []types.Message, queueURL string) {
   309	        const maxBatchSize = 10
   310	
   311	        for start := 0; start < len(messages); start += maxBatchSize {
   312	                end := start + maxBatchSize
   313	                if end > len(messages) {
   314	                        end = len(messages)
   315	                }
   316	
   317	                batch := messages[start:end]
   318	                entries := make([]types.DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry, len(batch))
   319	                for i, msg := range batch {
   320	                        entries[i] = types.DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry{
   321	                                Id:            aws.String(aws.ToString(msg.MessageId)),
   322	                                ReceiptHandle: msg.ReceiptHandle,
   323	                        }
   324	                }
   325	
   326	                input := &sqs.DeleteMessageBatchInput{
   327	                        QueueUrl: aws.String(queueURL),
   328	                        Entries:  entries,
   329	                }
   330	
   331	                output, err := c.client.DeleteMessageBatch(ctx, input)
   332	                if err != nil {
   333	                        c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("batch deletion failed")
   334	                        continue
   335	                }
   336	
   337	                if len(output.Failed) > 0 {
   338	                        for _, failed := range output.Failed {
   339	                                c.common.log.Error().Msgf("failed to delete message ID %s: %s", aws.ToString(failed.Id), aws.ToString(failed.Message))
   340	                        }
   341	                }
   342	        }
   343	}
   344	
   345	func getBoolAttributeValue(msg types.Message, key string) (bool, error) {
   346	        if attr, ok := msg.MessageAttributes[key]; ok && attr.StringValue != nil {
   347	                return strconv.ParseBool(*attr.StringValue)
   348	        }
   349	        return false, nil
   350	}
   351	
   352	func getUint64AttributeValue(msg types.Message, key string) (uint64, error) {
   353	        if attr, ok := msg.MessageAttributes[key]; ok && attr.StringValue != nil {
   354	                value, err := strconv.ParseUint(*attr.StringValue, 10, 64)
   355	                if err != nil {
   356	                        return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse %s attribute: %w", key, err)
   357	                }
   358	                return value, nil
   359	        }
   360	        return 0, nil
   361	}
   362	
   363	// processPublicationDataMessage handles messages in publication data mode.
   364	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) processPublicationDataMessage(ctx context.Context, msg types.Message, data []byte) error {
   365	        channelsAttr := getMessageAttributeValue(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.ChannelsAttribute)
   366	        if channelsAttr == "" {
   367	                c.common.log.Info().Msg("no channels found, skipping message")
   368	                return nil
   369	        }
   370	        idempotencyKey := getMessageAttributeValue(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.IdempotencyKeyAttribute)
   371	        delta, err := getBoolAttributeValue(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.DeltaAttribute)
   372	        if err != nil {
   373	                c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error parsing delta attribute, skipping message")
   374	                return nil // Skip message on parsing error.
   375	        }
   376	        channels := strings.Split(channelsAttr, ",")
   377	        tags := publicationTagsFromMessageAttributes(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.TagsAttributePrefix)
   378	        version, err := getUint64AttributeValue(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.VersionAttribute)
   379	        if err != nil {
   380	                c.common.log.Error().Err(err).Msg("error parsing version attribute, skipping message")
   381	                return nil // Skip message on parsing error.
   382	        }
   383	        return c.dispatcher.DispatchPublication(ctx, channels, api.ConsumedPublication{
   384	                Data:           data,
   385	                IdempotencyKey: idempotencyKey,
   386	                Delta:          delta,
   387	                Tags:           tags,
   388	                Version:        version,
   389	                VersionEpoch:   getMessageAttributeValue(msg, c.config.PublicationDataMode.VersionEpochAttribute),
   390	        })
   391	}
   392	
   393	// processCommandMessage handles non-publication messages.
   394	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) processCommandMessage(ctx context.Context, msg types.Message, data []byte) error {
   395	        method := getMessageAttributeValue(msg, c.config.MethodAttribute)
   396	        return c.dispatcher.DispatchCommand(ctx, method, data)
   397	}
   398	
   399	// extractMessageData returns the payload to be dispatched.
   400	// If the provider is "sns", it decodes the SNS envelope.
   401	func (c *AwsSqsConsumer) extractMessageData(msg *types.Message) ([]byte, error) {
   402	        if c.config.SNSEnvelope {
   403	                var envelope struct {
   404	                        Message string `json:"Message"`
   405	                }
   406	                if msg.Body == nil {
   407	                        return nil, errors.New("empty message body")
   408	                }
   409	                if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(*msg.Body), &envelope); err != nil {
   410	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal SNS envelope: %w", err)
   411	                }
   412	                return []byte(envelope.Message), nil
   413	        }
   414	        if msg.Body == nil {
   415	                return nil, errors.New("empty message body")
   416	        }
   417	        return []byte(*msg.Body), nil
   418	}
   419	
   420	// getMessageAttributeValue extracts the string value of the given attribute key from the SQS message.
   421	func getMessageAttributeValue(msg types.Message, key string) string {
   422	        if key == "" {
   423	                return ""
   424	        }
   425	        if attr, ok := msg.MessageAttributes[key]; ok && attr.StringValue != nil {
   426	                return *attr.StringValue
   427	        }
   428	        return ""
   429	}
   430	
   431	// publicationTagsFromMessageAttributes extracts tag values from message attributes using the given prefix.
   432	func publicationTagsFromMessageAttributes(msg types.Message, prefix string) map[string]string {
   433	        var tags map[string]string
   434	        if prefix == "" {
   435	                return tags
   436	        }
   437	        for k, v := range msg.MessageAttributes {
   438	                if strings.HasPrefix(k, prefix) && v.StringValue != nil {
   439	                        if tags == nil {
   440	                                tags = make(map[string]string)
   441	                        }
   442	                        tags[strings.TrimPrefix(k, prefix)] = *v.StringValue
   443	                }
   444	        }
   445	        return tags
   446	}

   791	type KafkaConsumerConfig struct {
   792	        Brokers        []string `mapstructure:"brokers" json:"brokers" envconfig:"brokers" yaml:"brokers" toml:"brokers"`
   793	        Topics         []string `mapstructure:"topics" json:"topics" envconfig:"topics" yaml:"topics" toml:"topics"`
   794	        ConsumerGroup  string   `mapstructure:"consumer_group" json:"consumer_group" envconfig:"consumer_group" yaml:"consumer_group" toml:"consumer_group"`
   795	        MaxPollRecords int      `mapstructure:"max_poll_records" json:"max_poll_records" envconfig:"max_poll_records" default:"100" yaml:"max_poll_records" toml:"max_poll_records"`
   796	        // FetchMaxBytes is the maximum number of bytes to fetch from Kafka in a single request.
   797	        // If not set the default 50MB is used.
   798	        FetchMaxBytes int32 `mapstructure:"fetch_max_bytes" json:"fetch_max_bytes" envconfig:"fetch_max_bytes" yaml:"fetch_max_bytes" toml:"fetch_max_bytes"`
   799	        // FetchMaxWait is the maximum time to wait for records when polling.
   800	        // If not set, defaults to 500ms.
   801	        FetchMaxWait Duration `mapstructure:"fetch_max_wait" json:"fetch_max_wait" envconfig:"fetch_max_wait" default:"500ms" yaml:"fetch_max_wait" toml:"fetch_max_wait"`
   802	        // FetchReadUncommitted is a flag to enable reading uncommitted messages from Kafka. By default, this is false and Centrifugo uses ReadCommitted mode.
   803	        FetchReadUncommitted bool `mapstructure:"fetch_read_uncommitted" json:"fetch_read_uncommitted" envconfig:"fetch_read_uncommitted" default:"false" yaml:"fetch_read_uncommitted" toml:"fetch_read_uncommitted"`
   804	        // PartitionQueueMaxSize is the maximum number of items in partition queue before pausing consuming from a partition.
   805	        // The actual queue size may exceed this value on `max_poll_records`, so this acts more like a threshold.
   806	        // If -1, pausing is done on every poll. If set, pausing only happens when queue size exceeds this threshold.
   807	        PartitionQueueMaxSize int `mapstructure:"partition_queue_max_size" json:"partition_queue_max_size" envconfig:"partition_queue_max_size" default:"1000" yaml:"partition_queue_max_size" toml:"partition_queue_max_size"`

   810	        TLS TLSConfig `mapstructure:"tls" json:"tls" envconfig:"tls" yaml:"tls" toml:"tls"`

   818	        // When set, enables the static membership protocol: during rolling restarts, a replacement
   819	        // consumer with the same instance ID takes over partitions seamlessly without triggering
   820	        // a group rebalance. This should be a stable identifier across restarts (e.g. Kubernetes pod name).
   821	        // When empty (default), static membership is not used.
   822	        InstanceID string `mapstructure:"instance_id" json:"instance_id" envconfig:"instance_id" yaml:"instance_id" toml:"instance_id"`

   833	func (c KafkaConsumerConfig) Validate() error {
   834	        if len(c.Brokers) == 0 {
   835	                return errors.New("no Kafka brokers provided")
   836	        }
   837	        if len(c.Topics) == 0 {
   838	                return errors.New("no Kafka topics provided")
   839	        }
   840	        if c.ConsumerGroup == "" {
   841	                return errors.New("no Kafka consumer group provided")
   842	        }
   843	        if c.PublicationDataMode.Enabled && c.PublicationDataMode.ChannelsHeader == "" {
   844	                return errors.New("no Kafka channels_header_name provided for publication data mode")
   845	        }
   846	        return nil
   847	}
   848	
   849	// KafkaPublicationDataModeConfig is a configuration for Kafka publication data mode.
   850	// In this mode we expect Kafka message payload to contain data ready to publish into
   851	// channels, instead of API command. All other fields used to build channel Publication
   852	// can be passed in Kafka message headers – thus it's possible to integrate existing
   853	// topics with Centrifugo.
   854	type KafkaPublicationDataModeConfig struct {
   855	        // Enabled enables Kafka publication data mode for the Kafka consumer.
   856	        Enabled bool `mapstructure:"enabled" json:"enabled" envconfig:"enabled" yaml:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
   857	        // ChannelsHeader is a header name to extract channels to publish data into
   858	        // (channels must be comma-separated). Ex. of value: "channel1,channel2".
   859	        ChannelsHeader string `mapstructure:"channels_header" default:"centrifugo-channels" json:"channels_header" envconfig:"channels_header" yaml:"channels_header" toml:"channels_header"`
   860	        // IdempotencyKeyHeader is a header name to extract Publication idempotency key from
   861	        // Kafka message. See https://centrifugal.dev/docs/server/server_api#publishrequest.
   862	        IdempotencyKeyHeader string `mapstructure:"idempotency_key_header" default:"centrifugo-idempotency-key" json:"idempotency_key_header" envconfig:"idempotency_key_header" yaml:"idempotency_key_header" toml:"idempotency_key_header"`
   863	        // DeltaHeader is a header name to extract Publication delta flag from Kafka message
   864	        // which tells Centrifugo whether to use delta compression for message or not.
   865	        // See https://centrifugal.dev/docs/server/delta_compression and
   866	        // https://centrifugal.dev/docs/server/server_api#publishrequest.
   867	        DeltaHeader string `mapstructure:"delta_header" default:"centrifugo-delta" json:"delta_header" envconfig:"delta_header" yaml:"delta_header" toml:"delta_header"`
   868	        // VersionHeader is a header name to extract Publication version from Kafka message.
   869	        VersionHeader string `mapstructure:"version_header" default:"centrifugo-version" json:"version_header" envconfig:"version_header" yaml:"version_header" toml:"version_header"`

  1133	// AwsSqsConsumerConfig holds configuration for the AWS consumer.
  1134	type AwsSqsConsumerConfig struct {
  1135	        // Queues is a list of SQS queue URLs to consume.
  1136	        Queues []string `mapstructure:"queues" json:"queues" envconfig:"queues" yaml:"queues" toml:"queues"`
  1137	        // SNSEnvelope, when true, expects messages to be wrapped in an SNS envelope – this is required when
  1138	        // consuming from SNS topics with SQS subscriptions.
  1139	        SNSEnvelope bool `mapstructure:"sns_envelope" json:"sns_envelope" envconfig:"sns_envelope" yaml:"sns_envelope" toml:"sns_envelope"`
  1140	        // Region is the AWS region.
  1141	        Region string `mapstructure:"region" json:"region" envconfig:"region" yaml:"region" toml:"region"`
  1142	        // MaxNumberOfMessages is the maximum number of messages to receive per poll.
  1143	        MaxNumberOfMessages int32 `mapstructure:"max_number_of_messages" default:"10" json:"max_number_of_messages" envconfig:"max_number_of_messages" yaml:"max_number_of_messages" toml:"max_number_of_messages"`
  1144	        // PollWaitTime is the long-poll wait time. Rounded to seconds internally.
  1145	        PollWaitTime Duration `mapstructure:"wait_time_time" json:"wait_time_time" envconfig:"wait_time_time" default:"20s" yaml:"wait_time_time" toml:"wait_time_time"`
  1146	        // VisibilityTimeout is the time a message is hidden from other consumers. Rounded to seconds internally.
  1147	        VisibilityTimeout Duration `mapstructure:"visibility_timeout" json:"visibility_timeout" envconfig:"visibility_timeout" default:"30s" yaml:"visibility_timeout" toml:"visibility_timeout"`
  1148	        // MaxConcurrency defines max concurrency during message batch processing.
  1149	        MaxConcurrency int `mapstructure:"max_concurrency" json:"max_concurrency" envconfig:"max_concurrency" default:"1" yaml:"max_concurrency" toml:"max_concurrency"`
  1150	        // CredentialsProfile is a shared credentials profile to use.
  1151	        CredentialsProfile string `mapstructure:"credentials_profile" json:"credentials_profile" envconfig:"credentials_profile" yaml:"credentials_profile" toml:"credentials_profile"`
  1152	        // AssumeRoleARN, if provided, will cause the consumer to assume the given IAM role.
  1153	        AssumeRoleARN string `mapstructure:"assume_role_arn" json:"assume_role_arn" envconfig:"assume_role_arn" yaml:"assume_role_arn" toml:"assume_role_arn"`
  1154	        // MethodAttribute is the attribute name to extract a method for command messages.
  1155	        // If provided in message, then payload must be just a serialized API request object.
  1156	        MethodAttribute string `mapstructure:"method_attribute" default:"centrifugo-method" json:"method_attribute" envconfig:"method_attribute" yaml:"method_attribute" toml:"method_attribute"`
  1157	        // LocalStackEndpoint if set enables using localstack with provided URL.
  1158	        LocalStackEndpoint string `mapstructure:"localstack_endpoint" json:"localstack_endpoint" envconfig:"localstack_endpoint" yaml:"localstack_endpoint" toml:"localstack_endpoint"`
  1159	        // PublicationDataMode holds settings for the mode where message payload already contains data
  1160	        // ready to publish into channels.
  1161	        PublicationDataMode AWSPublicationDataModeConfig `mapstructure:"publication_data_mode" json:"publication_data_mode" envconfig:"publication_data_mode" yaml:"publication_data_mode" toml:"publication_data_mode"`
  1162	}
  1163	
  1164	// AWSPublicationDataModeConfig holds configuration for the publication data mode.
  1165	type AWSPublicationDataModeConfig struct {
  1166	        // Enabled enables publication data mode.
  1167	        Enabled bool `mapstructure:"enabled" json:"enabled" envconfig:"enabled" yaml:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
  1168	        // ChannelsAttribute is the attribute name containing comma-separated channel names.
  1169	        ChannelsAttribute string `mapstructure:"channels_attribute" default:"centrifugo-channels" json:"channels_attribute" envconfig:"channels_attribute" yaml:"channels_attribute" toml:"channels_attribute"`
  1170	        // IdempotencyKeyAttribute is the attribute name for an idempotency key.
  1171	        IdempotencyKeyAttribute string `mapstructure:"idempotency_key_attribute" default:"centrifugo-idempotency-key" json:"idempotency_key_attribute" envconfig:"idempotency_key_attribute" yaml:"idempotency_key_attribute" toml:"idempotency_key_attribute"`
  1172	        // DeltaAttribute is the attribute name for a delta flag.
  1173	        DeltaAttribute string `mapstructure:"delta_attribute" default:"centrifugo-delta" json:"delta_attribute" envconfig:"delta_attribute" yaml:"delta_attribute" toml:"delta_attribute"`
  1174	        // VersionAttribute is the attribute name for a version of publication.
  1175	        VersionAttribute string `mapstructure:"version_attribute" default:"centrifugo-version" json:"version_attribute" envconfig:"version_attribute" yaml:"version_attribute" toml:"version_attribute"`
  1176	        // VersionEpochAttribute is the attribute name for a version epoch of publication.
  1177	        VersionEpochAttribute string `mapstructure:"version_epoch_attribute" default:"centrifugo-version-epoch" json:"version_epoch_attribute" envconfig:"version_epoch_attribute" yaml:"version_epoch_attribute" toml:"version_epoch_attribute"`
  1178	        // TagsAttributePrefix is the prefix for attributes containing tags.
  1179	        TagsAttributePrefix string `mapstructure:"tags_attribute_prefix" default:"centrifugo-tag-" json:"tags_attribute_prefix" envconfig:"tags_attribute_prefix" yaml:"tags_attribute_prefix" toml:"tags_attribute_prefix"`
  1180	}

  1184	        if len(c.Queues) == 0 {

    30	// MockDispatcher implements the Dispatcher interface for testing.
    31	type MockDispatcher struct {
    32	        onDispatchCommand     func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error
    33	        onDispatchPublication func(ctx context.Context, channels []string, pub api.ConsumedPublication) error
    34	}
    35	
    36	func (m *MockDispatcher) DispatchCommand(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
    37	        return m.onDispatchCommand(ctx, method, data)
    38	}
    39	
    40	func (m *MockDispatcher) DispatchPublication(ctx context.Context, channels []string, pub api.ConsumedPublication) error {
    41	        return m.onDispatchPublication(ctx, channels, pub)
    42	}
    43	
    44	func produceTestMessage(topic string, message []byte, headers []kgo.RecordHeader) error {
    45	        // Create a new client
    46	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL))
    47	        if err != nil {
    48	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to create Kafka client: %w", err)
    49	        }
    50	        defer client.Close()
    51	
    52	        // Produce a message
    53	        err = client.ProduceSync(context.Background(), &kgo.Record{
    54	                Topic:     topic,
    55	                Partition: 0,
    56	                Value:     message,
    57	                Headers:   headers,
    58	        }).FirstErr()
    59	        if err != nil {
    60	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to produce message: %w", err)
    61	        }
    62	        return nil
    63	}
    64	
    65	func produceManyRecords(records ...*kgo.Record) error {
    66	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL))
    67	        if err != nil {
    68	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to create Kafka client: %w", err)
    69	        }
    70	        defer client.Close()
    71	        err = client.ProduceSync(context.Background(), records...).FirstErr()
    72	        if err != nil {
    73	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to produce message: %w", err)
    74	        }
    75	        return nil
    76	}
    77	
    78	func produceTestMessageToPartition(topic string, message []byte, partition int32) error {
    79	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(
    80	                kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL),
    81	                kgo.RecordPartitioner(kgo.ManualPartitioner()),
    82	        )
    83	        if err != nil {
    84	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to create Kafka client: %w", err)
    85	        }
    86	        defer client.Close()
    87	
    88	        res := client.ProduceSync(context.Background(), &kgo.Record{
    89	                Topic: topic, Partition: partition, Value: message})
    90	        if res.FirstErr() != nil {
    91	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to produce message: %w", err)
    92	        }
    93	        producedPartition := res[0].Record.Partition
    94	        if producedPartition != partition {
    95	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to produce message to partition %d, produced to %d", partition, producedPartition)
    96	        }
    97	        return nil
    98	}
    99	
   100	func createTestTopic(ctx context.Context, topicName string, numPartitions int32, replicationFactor int16) error {
   101	        cl, err := kgo.NewClient(kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL))
   102	        if err != nil {
   103	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to create Kafka client: %w", err)
   104	        }
   105	        defer cl.Close()
   106	
   107	        client := kadm.NewClient(cl)
   108	        defer client.Close()
   109	
   110	        // Create the topic
   111	        resp, err := client.CreateTopics(ctx, numPartitions, replicationFactor, nil, topicName)
   112	        if err != nil {
   113	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to create topic: %w", err)
   114	        }
   115	
   116	        for _, topic := range resp.Sorted() {
   117	                if topic.Err != nil {
   118	                        if strings.Contains(topic.Err.Error(), "TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS") {
   119	                                continue
   120	                        }
   121	                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to create topic '%s': %v", topic.Topic, topic.Err)
   122	                }
   123	        }
   124	        return nil
   125	}
   126	
   127	func waitCh(t *testing.T, ch chan struct{}, timeout time.Duration, failureMessage string) {
   128	        t.Helper()
   129	        select {
   130	        case <-ch:
   131	        case <-time.After(timeout):
   132	                require.Fail(t, failureMessage)
   133	        }
   134	}
   135	
   136	func waitAnyCh(t *testing.T, channels []chan struct{}, timeout time.Duration, failureMessage string) {
   137	        t.Helper()
   138	
   139	        anyCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
   140	
   141	        for _, ch := range channels {
   142	                go func(c chan struct{}) {
   143	                        select {
   144	                        case <-c:
   145	                                select {
   146	                                case anyCh <- struct{}{}:
   147	                                default:
   148	                                }
   149	                        case <-time.After(timeout):
   150	                                // let main handle timeout
   151	                        }
   152	                }(ch)
   153	        }
   154	
   155	        select {
   156	        case <-anyCh:
   157	                // One of the channels received a signal
   158	        case <-time.After(timeout):
   159	                require.Fail(t, failureMessage)
   160	        }
   161	}

   163	func TestKafkaConsumer_GreenScenario(t *testing.T) {
   164	        t.Parallel()
   165	        testKafkaTopic := "centrifugo_consumer_test_" + uuid.New().String()
   166	        testMethod := "method"
   167	        testPayload := []byte(`{"key":"value"}`)
   168	
   169	        testEvent := api.MethodWithRequestPayload{
   170	                Method:  testMethod,
   171	                Payload: api.JSONRawOrString(testPayload),
   172	        }
   173	        testMessage, _ := json.Marshal(testEvent)
   174	
   175	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   176	        defer cancel()
   177	
   178	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic, 1, 1)
   179	        require.NoError(t, err)
   180	
   181	        config := KafkaConfig{
   182	                Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL}, // Adjust as needed
   183	                Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic},
   184	                ConsumerGroup: uuid.New().String(),
   185	        }
   186	
   187	        eventReceived := make(chan struct{})
   188	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   189	
   190	        consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(config, &MockDispatcher{
   191	                onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
   192	                        require.Equal(t, "", method)
   193	                        require.Equal(t, testMessage, data)
   194	                        close(eventReceived)
   195	                        return nil
   196	                },
   197	        }, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
   198	        require.NoError(t, err)
   199	
   200	        go func() {
   201	                err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   202	                require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   203	                close(consumerClosed)
   204	        }()
   205	
   206	        err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, testMessage, nil)
   207	        require.NoError(t, err)
   208	
   209	        waitCh(t, eventReceived, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for event")
   210	        cancel()
   211	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   212	}

   214	func TestKafkaConsumer_SeveralConsumers(t *testing.T) {
   215	        t.Parallel()
   216	        testKafkaTopic := "centrifugo_consumer_test_" + uuid.New().String()
   217	        testMethod := "method"
   218	        testPayload := []byte(`{"key":"value"}`)
   219	
   220	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   221	        defer cancel()
   222	
   223	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic, 1, 1)
   224	        require.NoError(t, err)
   225	
   226	        config := KafkaConfig{
   227	                Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL}, // Adjust as needed
   228	                Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic},
   229	                ConsumerGroup: uuid.New().String(),
   230	        }
   231	
   232	        eventReceived := make(chan struct{})
   233	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   234

   237	                Payload: api.JSONRawOrString(testPayload),
   238	        }

   244	                                require.Equal(t, testMessage, data)

   247	                        },
   248	                }, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))

   250

   252	                        err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   253	                        require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   254	                        consumerClosed <- struct{}{}
   255	                }()
   256	        }
   257	
   258	        err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, testMessage, nil)
   259	        require.NoError(t, err)
   260

   262	        cancel()
   263	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   264	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   265	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   266	}
