   268	func TestKafkaConsumer_RetryAfterDispatchError(t *testing.T) {
   269	        t.Parallel()
   270	        testKafkaTopic := "centrifugo_consumer_test_" + uuid.New().String()
   271	        testMethod := "method"
   272	        testPayload := []byte(`{"key":"value"}`)
   273	
   274	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   275	        defer cancel()
   276	
   277	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic, 1, 1)
   278	        require.NoError(t, err)
   279	
   280	        config := KafkaConfig{
   281	                Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
   282	                Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic},
   283	                ConsumerGroup: uuid.New().String(),
   284	        }
   285	
   286	        successCh := make(chan struct{})
   287	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   288	
   289	        retryCount := 0
   290	        numFailures := 3
   291	
   292	        mockDispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
   293	                onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
   294	                        if retryCount < numFailures {
   295	                                retryCount++
   296	                                return errors.New("dispatch error")
   297	                        }
   298	                        close(successCh)
   299	                        return nil
   300	                },
   301	        }
   302	        consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(
   303	                config,
   304	                mockDispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
   305	        require.NoError(t, err)
   306	
   307	        go func() {
   308	                err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   309	                require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   310	                close(consumerClosed)
   311	        }()
   312	
   313	        testEvent := api.MethodWithRequestPayload{
   314	                Method:  testMethod,
   315	                Payload: api.JSONRawOrString(testPayload),
   316	        }
   317	        testMessage, _ := json.Marshal(testEvent)
   318	        err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, testMessage, nil)
   319	        require.NoError(t, err)
   320	
   321	        waitCh(t, successCh, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for successful event process")
   322	        require.Equal(t, numFailures, retryCount)
   323	        cancel()
   324	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   325	}
   326	
   327	// In this test we simulate a scenario where a partition is blocked and the partition consumer
   328	// is stuck on it. We want to make sure that the consumer is not blocked and can still process
   329	// messages from other topics.
   330	func TestKafkaConsumer_BlockedPartitionDoesNotBlockAnotherTopic(t *testing.T) {
   331	        t.Parallel()
   332	        testKafkaTopic1 := "consumer_test_1_" + uuid.New().String()
   333	        testKafkaTopic2 := "consumer_test_1_" + uuid.New().String()
   334	
   335	        testPayload1 := []byte(`{"key":"value1"}`)
   336	        testPayload2 := []byte(`{"key":"value2"}`)
   337	
   338	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   339	        defer cancel()
   340	
   341	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic1, 1, 1)
   342	        require.NoError(t, err)
   343	
   344	        err = createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic2, 1, 1)
   345	        require.NoError(t, err)
   346	
   347	        event1Received := make(chan struct{})
   348	        event2Received := make(chan struct{})
   349	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   350	        doneCh := make(chan struct{})
   351	
   352	        config := KafkaConfig{
   353	                Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
   354	                Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic1, testKafkaTopic2},
   355	                ConsumerGroup: uuid.New().String(),
   356	        }
   357	
   358	        numCalls := 0
   359	
   360	        mockDispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
   361	                onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
   362	                        if numCalls == 0 {
   363	                                numCalls++
   364	                                close(event1Received)
   365	                                // Block till the event2 received. This must not block the consumer and event2
   366	                                // must still be processed successfully.
   367	                                <-event2Received
   368	                                return nil
   369	                        }
   370	                        close(event2Received)
   371	                        return nil
   372	                },
   373	        }
   374	        consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(
   375	                config, mockDispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
   376	        require.NoError(t, err)
   377	
   378	        go func() {
   379	                err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic1, testPayload1, nil)
   380	                require.NoError(t, err)
   381	
   382	                // Wait until the first message is received to make sure messages read by separate PollRecords calls.
   383	                <-event1Received
   384	                err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic2, testPayload2, nil)
   385	                require.NoError(t, err)
   386	        }()
   387	
   388	        go func() {
   389	                err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   390	                require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   391	                close(consumerClosed)
   392	        }()
   393	
   394	        waitCh(t, event2Received, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for event 2")
   395	        cancel()
   396	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   397	        close(doneCh)
   398	}
   399	
   400	// In this test we simulate a scenario where a partition is blocked and the partition consumer
   401	// is stuck on it. We want to make sure that the consumer is not blocked and can still process
   402	// messages from other topic partitions.
   403	func TestKafkaConsumer_BlockedPartitionDoesNotBlockAnotherPartition(t *testing.T) {
   404	        t.Parallel()
   405	        testKafkaTopic1 := "consumer_test_1_" + uuid.New().String()
   406	
   407	        testPayload1 := []byte(`{"key":"value1"}`)
   408	        testPayload2 := []byte(`{"key":"value2"}`)
   409	
   410	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   411	        defer cancel()
   412	
   413	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic1, 2, 1)
   414	        require.NoError(t, err)
   415	
   416	        event1Received := make(chan struct{})
   417	        event2Received := make(chan struct{})
   418	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   419	        doneCh := make(chan struct{})
   420	
   421	        config := KafkaConfig{
   422	                Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
   423	                Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic1},
   424	                ConsumerGroup: uuid.New().String(),
   425	        }
   426	
   427	        numCalls := 0
   428	
   429	        mockDispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
   430	                onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
   431	                        if numCalls == 0 {
   432	                                numCalls++
   433	                                close(event1Received)
   434	                                // Block till the event2 received. This must not block the consumer and event2
   435	                                // must still be processed successfully.
   436	                                <-event2Received
   437	                                return nil
   438	                        }
   439	                        close(event2Received)
   440	                        return nil
   441	                },
   442	        }
   443	        consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(
   444	                config, mockDispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
   445	        require.NoError(t, err)
   446	
   447	        go func() {
   448	                err = produceTestMessageToPartition(testKafkaTopic1, testPayload1, 0)
   449	                require.NoError(t, err)
   450	
   451	                // Wait until the first message is received to make sure messages read by separate PollRecords calls.
   452	                <-event1Received
   453	                err = produceTestMessageToPartition(testKafkaTopic1, testPayload2, 1)
   454	                require.NoError(t, err)
   455	        }()
   456	
   457	        go func() {
   458	                err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   459	                require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   460	                close(consumerClosed)
   461	        }()
   462	
   463	        waitCh(t, event2Received, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for event 2")
   464	        cancel()
   465	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   466	        close(doneCh)
   467	}
   468	
   469	func TestKafkaConsumer_PausePartitions(t *testing.T) {
   470	        t.Parallel()
   471	        testKafkaTopic := "consumer_test_" + uuid.New().String()
   472	        testPayload1 := []byte(`{"key":"value1"}`)
   473	        testPayload2 := []byte(`{"key":"value2"}`)
   474	
   475	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   476	        defer cancel()
   477	
   478	        err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic, 1, 1)
   479	        require.NoError(t, err)
   480	
   481	        event1Received := make(chan struct{})
   482	        event2Received := make(chan struct{})
   483	        consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
   484	        doneCh := make(chan struct{})
   485	
   486	        fetchSubmittedCh := make(chan kgo.FetchTopicPartition)
   487	        beforePauseCh := make(chan topicPartition)
   488	
   489	        config := KafkaConfig{
   490	                Brokers:               []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
   491	                Topics:                []string{testKafkaTopic},
   492	                ConsumerGroup:         uuid.New().String(),
   493	                PartitionQueueMaxSize: -1,
   494	        }
   495	
   496	        numCalls := 0
   497	
   498	        unblockCh := make(chan struct{})
   499	
   500	        testConfig := testOnlyConfig{
   501	                fetchTopicPartitionSubmittedCh: fetchSubmittedCh,
   502	                topicPartitionBeforePauseCh:    beforePauseCh,
   503	        }
   504	
   505	        mockDispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
   506	                onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
   507	                        numCalls++
   508	                        if numCalls == 1 {
   509	                                close(event1Received)
   510	                                <-unblockCh
   511	                                return nil
   512	                        }
   513	                        close(event2Received)
   514	                        return nil
   515	                },
   516	        }
   517	        consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(
   518	                config, mockDispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
   519	        require.NoError(t, err)
   520	
   521	        consumer.testOnlyConfig = testConfig
   522	
   523	        go func() {
   524	                err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, testPayload1, nil)
   525	                require.NoError(t, err)
   526	                <-beforePauseCh // Wait for triggering the partition pause.
   527	                <-fetchSubmittedCh
   528	                <-event1Received
   529	                // Unblock the message processing.
   530	                close(unblockCh)
   531	
   532	                // At this point message 1 is being processed and the next produced message must
   533	                // cause a partition pause.
   534	                err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, testPayload2, nil)
   535	                require.NoError(t, err)
   536	                <-beforePauseCh // Wait for triggering the partition pause.
   537	                <-fetchSubmittedCh
   538	        }()
   539	
   540	        go func() {
   541	                err := consumer.Run(ctx)
   542	                require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
   543	                close(consumerClosed)
   544	        }()
   545	
   546	        waitCh(t, event1Received, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for event 1")
   547	        waitCh(t, event2Received, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for event 2")
   548	        cancel()
   549	        waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer closed")
   550	        close(doneCh)
   551	}
   552

     2	// Java source.
     3	//
     4	// The Java source can be found at https://github.com/aws/aws-msk-iam-auth.

    25	// Auth contains an AWS AccessKey and SecretKey for authentication.
    26	//
    27	// This client may add fields to this struct in the future if Kafka adds more
    28	// capabilities to MSK IAM.
    29	type Auth struct {
    30	        // AccessKey is an AWS AccessKey.
    31	        AccessKey string
    32	
    33	        // AccessKey is an AWS SecretKey.
    34	        SecretKey string
    35	
    36	        // SessionToken, if non-empty, is a session / security token to use for
    37	        // authentication.
    38	        //
    39	        // See the following link for more details:
    40	        //
    41	        //     https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/welcome.html
    42	        //
    43	        SessionToken string
    44	
    45	        // UserAgent is the user agent to for the client to use when connecting
    46	        // to Kafka, overriding the default "franz-go/<runtime.Version()>/<hostname>".
    47	        //
    48	        // Setting a UserAgent allows authorizing based on the aws:UserAgent
    49	        // condition key; see the following link for more details:
    50	        //
    51	        //     https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/reference_policies_condition-keys.html#condition-keys-useragent
    52	        //
    53	        UserAgent string
    54	
    55	        _ struct{} // require explicit field initialization
    56	}
    57	
    58	var hostname, _ = os.Hostname()
    59	
    60	func init() {
    61	        if hostname == "" {
    62	                hostname = "unknown"
    63	        }
    64	}
    65	
    66	// AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism returns a sasl mechanism that will use 'a' as
    67	// credentials for all sasl sessions.
    68	//
    69	// This is a shortcut for using the ManagedStreamingIAM function and is useful
    70	// when you do not need to live-rotate credentials.
    71	func (a Auth) AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism() sasl.Mechanism {
    72	        return ManagedStreamingIAM(func(context.Context) (Auth, error) {
    73	                return a, nil
    74	        })
    75	}
    76	
    77	type mskiam func(context.Context) (Auth, error)
    78	
    79	// ManagedStreamingIAM returns a sasl mechanism that will call authFn whenever
    80	// sasl authentication is needed. The returned Auth is used for a single
    81	// session.
    82	func ManagedStreamingIAM(authFn func(context.Context) (Auth, error)) sasl.Mechanism {
    83	        return mskiam(authFn)
    84	}
    85	
    86	func (mskiam) Name() string { return "AWS_MSK_IAM" }
    87	
    88	func (fn mskiam) Authenticate(ctx context.Context, host string) (sasl.Session, []byte, error) {
    89	        auth, err := fn(ctx)
    90	        if err != nil {
    91	                return nil, nil, err
    92	        }
    93	
    94	        challenge, err := challenge(auth, host)
    95	        if err != nil {
    96	                return nil, nil, err
    97	        }
    98	
    99	        return new(session), challenge, nil
   100	}
   101	
   102	type session struct{}
   103	
   104	func (session) Challenge(resp []byte) (bool, []byte, error) {
   105	        if len(resp) == 0 {
   106	                return false, nil, errors.New("empty challenge response: failed")
   107	        }
   108	        return true, nil, nil
   109	}
   110	
   111	const service = "kafka-cluster"
   112	
   113	func challenge(auth Auth, host string) ([]byte, error) {
   114	        host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(host) // we do not need the port
   115	        if err != nil {
   116	                return nil, err
   117	        }
   118	        region, err := identifyRegion(host)
   119	        if err != nil {
   120	                return nil, err
   121	        }
   122	
   123	        var (
   124	                timestamp = time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
   125	                date      = timestamp[:8] // 20060102
   126	                scope     = scope(date, region)
   127	                v         = make(url.Values)
   128	        )
   129	
   130	        v.Set("Action", service+":Connect")
   131	        v.Set("X-Amz-Algorithm", "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256")
   132	        v.Set("X-Amz-Credential", auth.AccessKey+"/"+scope)
   133	        v.Set("X-Amz-Date", timestamp)
   134	        v.Set("X-Amz-Expires", "300") // 5 min
   135	        v.Set("X-Amz-SignedHeaders", "host")
   136	        if auth.SessionToken != "" {
   137	                v.Set("X-Amz-Security-Token", auth.SessionToken)
   138	        }
   139	
   140	        qps := strings.ReplaceAll(v.Encode(), "+", "%20")
   141	
   142	        canonicalRequest := task1(host, qps)
   143	        sts := task2(timestamp, scope, canonicalRequest)
   144	        signature := task3(auth.SecretKey, region, date, sts)
   145	
   146	        v.Set("X-Amz-Signature", signature) // task4
   147	
   148	        // According to the Java source and manual testing, all values in our
   149	        // challenge map must be lowercased, and we MUST have host, and we MUST
   150	        // have version, and version MUST be 2020_10_22.
   151	        keyvals := make(map[string]string)
   152	        for key, values := range v {
   153	                keyvals[strings.ToLower(key)] = values[0]
   154	        }
   155	        keyvals["host"] = host
   156	        keyvals["version"] = "2020_10_22"
   157	        ua := auth.UserAgent
   158	        if ua == "" {
   159	                ua = strings.Join([]string{"franz-go", runtime.Version(), hostname}, "/")
   160	        }
   161	        keyvals["user-agent"] = ua
   162	
   163	        marshaled, err := json.Marshal(keyvals)
   164	        if err != nil {
   165	                return nil, err
   166	        }
   167	        return marshaled, nil
   168	}
   169	
   170	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-string-to-sign.html
   171	// "CredentialScope", Part 3
   172	func scope(date, region string) string {
   173	        return strings.Join([]string{date, region, service, "aws4_request"}, "/")
   174	}
   175	
   176	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-canonical-request.html
   177	func task1(host, qps string) []byte {
   178	        // We start with our defined method, "GET", and the defined empty path,
   179	        // "/". For query parameters, we have to escape +'s with %20, but we did
   180	        // that above when building our URL.
   181	        //
   182	        //   HTTPRequestMethod + '\n' +
   183	        //   CanonicalURI + '\n' +
   184	        //   CanonicalQueryString + '\n' +
   185	        canon := make([]byte, 0, 200)
   186	        canon = append(canon, "GET\n"...)
   187	        canon = append(canon, "/\n"...)
   188	        canon = append(canon, qps...)
   189	        canon = append(canon, '\n')
   190	
   191	        // We only sign one header, the host. Each signed header is followed by
   192	        // a newline, and then the canonical header block is followed itself by
   193	        // a newline.
   194	        //
   195	        //   CanonicalHeaders + '\n' +
   196	        //   SignedHeaders + '\n' +
   197	        canon = append(canon, "host:"...)
   198	        canon = append(canon, host...)
   199	        canon = append(canon, "\n\nhost\n"...)
   200	
   201	        // Finally, we add our empty body.
   202	        //
   203	        //   HexEncode(Hash(RequestPayload))
   204	        const emptyBody = "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"
   205	        return append(canon, emptyBody...)
   206	}
   207	
   208	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-string-to-sign.html
   209	func task2(timestamp, scope string, canonicalRequest []byte) []byte {
   210	        toSign := make([]byte, 0, 512)
   211	        toSign = append(toSign, "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\n"...)
   212	        toSign = append(toSign, timestamp...)
   213	        toSign = append(toSign, '\n')
   214	        toSign = append(toSign, scope...)
   215	        toSign = append(toSign, '\n')
   216	        canonHash := sha256.Sum256(canonicalRequest)
   217	        hexBuf := make([]byte, 64) // 32 bytes to 64
   218	        hex.Encode(hexBuf, canonHash[:])
   219	        toSign = append(toSign, hexBuf...)
   220	        return toSign
   221	}
   222	
   223	var aws4requestBytes = []byte("aws4_request")
   224	
   225	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-calculate-signature.html
   226	func task3(secretKey, region, date string, sts []byte) string {
   227	        key := make([]byte, 0, 100)
   228	        key = append(key, "AWS4"...)
   229	        key = append(key, secretKey...)
   230	
   231	        h := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
   232	        h.Write([]byte(date)) // kDate
   233	
   234	        key = h.Sum(key[:0])
   235	        h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
   236	        h.Write([]byte(region)) // kRegion
   237	
   238	        key = h.Sum(key[:0])
   239	        h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
   240	        h.Write([]byte(service)) // kService
   241	
   242	        key = h.Sum(key[:0])
   243	        h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
   244	        h.Write(aws4requestBytes) // kSigning
   245	
   246	        key = h.Sum(key[:0])
   247	        h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
   248	        h.Write(sts)
   249	
   250	        return hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(key[:0]))
   251	}
   252	
   253	// aws-java-sdk-core/src/main/resources/com/amazonaws/partitions/endpoints.json
   254	var suffixes = []string{
   255	        ".amazonaws.com",
   256	        ".amazonaws.com.cn",
   257	        ".c2s.ic.gov",
   258	        ".sc2s.sgov.gov",
   259	}
   260	
   261	// aws-java-sdk-core/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/partitions/PartitionMetadataProvider.java
   262	// tryGetRegionByEndpointDnsSuffix
   263	func identifyRegion(host string) (string, error) {
   264	        for _, suffix := range suffixes {
   265	                if strings.HasSuffix(host, suffix) {
   266	                        serviceRegion := strings.TrimSuffix(host, suffix)
   267	                        regionDot := strings.LastIndexByte(serviceRegion, '.')
   268	                        if regionDot == -1 {
   269	                                break
   270	                        }
   271	                        return serviceRegion[regionDot+1:], nil
   272	                }
   273	        }
   274	        return "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine the region in %+q", host)
   275	}

2026-05-29 21:31:48,012 - INFO - swea-agent-centrifugal__centrifugo-1129 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I have a good understanding. The `aws.Auth` struct in franz-go supports:
- `AccessKey`
- `SecretKey`
- `SessionToken` (for STS credentials)

The key insight: The franz-go package has `ManagedStreamingIAM` which accepts a function that can be called each time authentication is needed. This means we can use a function that calls AWS STS AssumeRole to get fresh credentials.

But actually, looking at this more carefully, the simpler approach would be:
1. Add `AssumeRoleARN` and `CredentialsProfile` to `KafkaConsumerConfig`
2. When `aws-msk-iam` is used with `AssumeRoleARN`, use the AWS SDK to call STS AssumeRole and get temporary credentials (AccessKey, SecretKey, SessionToken) and pass them to `aws.Auth`

Let me check how the AWS SDK is already used in the project:

	// When set, enables the static membership protocol: during rolling restarts, a replacement
	// consumer with the same instance ID takes over partitions seamlessly without triggering
	// a group rebalance. This should be a stable identifier across restarts (e.g. Kubernetes pod name).
	// When empty (default), static membership is not used.
	InstanceID string `mapstructure:"instance_id" json:"instance_id" envconfig:"instance_id" yaml:"instance_id" toml:"instance_id"`

	// MethodHeader is a header name to extract method name from Kafka message.
	// If provided in message, then payload must be just a serialized API request object.
	MethodHeader string `mapstructure:"method_header" default:"centrifugo-method" json:"method_header" envconfig:"method_header" yaml:"method_header" toml:"method_header"`

	// PublicationDataMode is a configuration for the mode where message payload already
	// contains data ready to publish into channels, instead of API command.
	PublicationDataMode KafkaPublicationDataModeConfig `mapstructure:"publication_data_mode" json:"publication_data_mode" envconfig:"publication_data_mode" yaml:"publication_data_mode" toml:"publication_data_mode"`
}' --new_str '// InstanceID sets a stable consumer group instance ID for Kafka static membership.

	// When empty (default), static membership is not used.

	CredentialsProfile string `mapstructure:"credentials_profile" json:"credentials_profile" envconfig:"credentials_profile" yaml:"credentials_profile" toml:"credentials_profile"`

   165	
   166	func (c *KafkaConsumer) initClient() (*kgo.Client, error) {
   167	        opts := []kgo.Opt{
   168	                kgo.SeedBrokers(c.config.Brokers...),
   169	                kgo.ConsumeTopics(c.config.Topics...),
   170	                kgo.ConsumerGroup(c.config.ConsumerGroup),
   171	                kgo.OnPartitionsAssigned(c.assigned),
   172	                kgo.OnPartitionsRevoked(c.revoked),
   173	                kgo.OnPartitionsLost(c.lost),
   174	                kgo.AutoCommitMarks(),
   175	                kgo.BlockRebalanceOnPoll(),
   176	                kgo.ClientID(kafkaClientID),
   177	                kgo.FetchMaxWait(time.Duration(c.config.FetchMaxWait)),
   178	                kgo.WithLogger(&kgoZerologAdapter{log: c.common.log}),
   179	        }
   180	        if c.config.InstanceID != "" { // Important to keep separate, otherwise pointer to empty string is used in kgo.InstanceID.
   181	                opts = append(opts, kgo.InstanceID(c.config.InstanceID))
   182	        }
   183	        if c.config.FetchMaxBytes > 0 {
   184	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchMaxBytes(c.config.FetchMaxBytes))
   185	        }
   186	        if !c.config.FetchReadUncommitted {
   187	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadCommitted()))
   188	        } else {
   189	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadUncommitted()))
   190	        }
   191	        if c.config.TLS.Enabled {
   192	                tlsConfig, err := c.config.TLS.ToGoTLSConfig("kafka:" + c.name)
   193	                if err != nil {
   194	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("error making TLS configuration: %w", err)
   195	                }
   196	                dialer := &tls.Dialer{
   197	                        NetDialer: &net.Dialer{Timeout: 10 * time.Second},
   198	                        Config:    tlsConfig,
   199	                }
   200	                opts = append(opts, kgo.Dialer(dialer.DialContext))
   201	        }
   202	
   203	        if c.config.SASLMechanism != "" {
   204	                switch c.config.SASLMechanism {
   205	                case "plain":
   206	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(plain.Auth{
   207	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   208	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   209	                        }.AsMechanism()))
   210	                case "scram-sha-256":
   211	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   212	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   213	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   214	                        }.AsSha256Mechanism()))
   215	                case "scram-sha-512":
   216	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   217	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   218	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   219	                        }.AsSha512Mechanism()))
   220	                case "aws-msk-iam":
   221	                        awsAuth, err := c.resolveAwsAuth()
   222	                        if err != nil {
   223	                                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error resolving AWS auth: %w", err)
   224	                        }
   225	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(awsAuth.AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()))
   226	                default:
   227	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported SASL mechanism: %s", c.config.SASLMechanism)
   228	                }
   229	        }
   230	
   231	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(opts...)
   232	        if err != nil {
   233	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error initializing client: %w", err)
   234	        }
   235	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   236	        defer cancel()
   237	        if err := client.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
   238	                client.Close()
   239	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error ping Kafka: %w", err)
   240	        }
   241	        return client, nil
   242	}

// resolveAwsAuth resolves AWS credentials for MSK IAM authentication.
// If AssumeRoleARN is provided, it uses STS to assume the role and obtain temporary credentials.
// If CredentialsProfile is provided, it uses the specified shared credentials profile.
// Otherwise, it falls back to the SASL user/password fields for backwards compatibility.
func (c *KafkaConsumer) resolveAwsAuth() (franzaws.Auth, error) {
        // If AssumeRoleARN is provided, use STS AssumeRole to get temporary credentials.
        if c.config.AssumeRoleARN != "" {
                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
                defer cancel()

                loadOpts := []func(*awsconfig.LoadOptions) error{}
                if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
                        loadOpts = append(loadOpts, awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile))
                }

                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, loadOpts...)
                if err != nil {
                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config: %w", err)
                }

                stsClient := sts.NewFromConfig(awsCfg)
                assumeRoleProvider := stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider(stsClient, c.config.AssumeRoleARN)

                creds, err := assumeRoleProvider.Retrieve(ctx)
                if err != nil {
                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to assume role: %w", err)
                }

                return franzaws.Auth{
                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
                }, nil
        }

        // If CredentialsProfile is provided (but no AssumeRoleARN), load credentials from the profile.
        if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
                defer cancel()

                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx,
                        awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile),
                )
                if err != nil {
                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config with profile: %w", err)
                }

                creds, err := awsCfg.Credentials.Retrieve(ctx)
                if err != nil {
                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to retrieve credentials from profile: %w", err)
                }

                return franzaws.Auth{
                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
                }, nil
        }

        // Fall back to SASL user/password for backwards compatibility.
        return franzaws.Auth{
                AccessKey: c.config.SASLUser,
                SecretKey: c.config.SASLPassword,
        }, nil
}

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/internal/consuming/kafka.go:
   244	// If AssumeRoleARN is provided, it uses STS to assume the role and obtain temporary credentials.
   245	// If CredentialsProfile is provided, it uses the specified shared credentials profile.
   246	// Otherwise, it falls back to the SASL user/password fields for backwards compatibility.
   247	func (c *KafkaConsumer) resolveAwsAuth() (franzaws.Auth, error) {
   248	        // If AssumeRoleARN is provided, use STS AssumeRole to get temporary credentials.
   249	        if c.config.AssumeRoleARN != "" {
   250	                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   251	                defer cancel()
   252	
   253	                loadOpts := []func(*awsconfig.LoadOptions) error{}
   254	                if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
   255	                        loadOpts = append(loadOpts, awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile))
   256	                }
   257	
   258	                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, loadOpts...)
   259	                if err != nil {
   260	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config: %w", err)
   261	                }
   262	
   263	                stsClient := sts.NewFromConfig(awsCfg)
   264	                assumeRoleProvider := stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider(stsClient, c.config.AssumeRoleARN)
   265	
   266	                creds, err := assumeRoleProvider.Retrieve(ctx)
   267	                if err != nil {
   268	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to assume role: %w", err)
   269	                }
   270	
   271	                return franzaws.Auth{
   272	                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
   273	                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
   274	                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
   275	                }, nil
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        // If CredentialsProfile is provided (but no AssumeRoleARN), load credentials from the profile.
   279	        if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
   280	                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   281	                defer cancel()
   282	
   283	                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx,
   284	                        awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile),
   285	                )
   286	                if err != nil {
   287	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config with profile: %w", err)
   288	                }
   289	
   290	                creds, err := awsCfg.Credentials.Retrieve(ctx)
   291	                if err != nil {
   292	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to retrieve credentials from profile: %w", err)
   293	                }
   294	
   295	                return franzaws.Auth{
   296	                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
   297	                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
   298	                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
   299	                }, nil
   300	        }
   301	
   302	        // Fall back to SASL user/password for backwards compatibility.
   303	        return franzaws.Auth{
   304	                AccessKey: c.config.SASLUser,
   305	                SecretKey: c.config.SASLPassword,
   306	        }, nil
   307	}
   308

   310	        defer func() {

Good. The old import was `"github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/aws"` and now it's aliased as `franzaws`. All references are updated.

Now let me think about edge cases:

1. **Backwards compatibility**: If neither `AssumeRoleARN` nor `CredentialsProfile` is set, it falls back to using `SASLUser`/`SASLPassword` as before. ✓
2. **AssumeRoleARN takes precedence**: If both `AssumeRoleARN` and direct keys are set, `AssumeRoleARN` is used. ✓
3. **CredentialsProfile without AssumeRoleARN**: Uses the profile to load credentials. ✓
4. **CredentialsProfile with AssumeRoleARN**: Uses the profile for the initial AWS config, then assumes the role. ✓
5. **SessionToken**: The STS assume role returns a session token, which is properly passed to the `franzaws.Auth` struct. ✓

Let me also verify the config types changes are consistent with the SQS consumer pattern

2026-05-29 21:36:04,488 - INFO - swea-agent-centrifugal__centrifugo-1129 - 💭 THOUGHT
The config structure matches the SQS consumer perfectly.

Now, I want to double-check one more thing: the SQS consumer uses `awsCfg.Credentials = aws.NewCredentialsCache(assumeRoleProvider)` to set the credentials on the AWS config. In our case, we directly call `Retrieve` on the provider. Both approaches should work, but let me make sure the `Retrieve` approach is correct.

Looking at the `stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider` documentation, it returns a `*AssumeRoleProvider` which implements the `aws.CredentialsProvider` interface. The `Retrieve` method should work to get a single set of credentials. This is appropriate for our use case since we're using it during initialization. However, there's a subtlety: credentials may expire. 

For a more robust implementation, we could use `ManagedStreamingIAM` instead of `AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism`. The `ManagedStreamingIAM` function takes a callback that is called each time authentication is needed, which would allow us to refresh credentials. But looking at the current codebase, the SQS consumer also just sets credentials once at initialization. And the original code also just sets credentials once. So for consistency, the current approach is fine.

However, I should consider using `ManagedStreamingIAM` for better reliability with temporary credentials. Let me look at how the franz-go library expects this to work:

The `AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()` method is a shortcut that returns the same credentials every time. For STS credentials that expire, we should use `ManagedStreamingIAM` which calls a function each time. But for now, to keep it simple and consistent with the existing pattern (and the SQS consumer which also doesn't auto-refresh), this is fine. The PR description says "use AWS STS (via the SDK) to obtain temporary credentials" - so getting them once at init time is acceptable.

Let me also verify that the code compiles correctly after reverting go.mod. Let me try a different approach to verify:

cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/test_kafka_auth.go << 'EOF'
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "github.com/centrifugal/centrifugo/v6/internal/configtypes"
)

func main() {
    // Test 1: Backwards compatibility - no assume role, uses SASL user/password
    cfg := configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig{
        Brokers:       []string{"localhost:9092"},
        Topics:        []string{"test"},
        ConsumerGroup: "test-group",
        SASLMechanism: "aws-msk-iam",
        SASLUser:      "AKIDEXAMPLE",
        SASLPassword:  "secretkey",
    }
    fmt.Printf("Test 1 - SASL User: %s, Password: %s\n", cfg.SASLUser, cfg.SASLPassword)
    fmt.Printf("Test 1 - AssumeRoleARN: '%s', CredentialsProfile: '%s'\n", cfg.AssumeRoleARN, cfg.CredentialsProfile)
    
    // Test 2: With AssumeRoleARN
    cfg2 := configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig{
        Brokers:       []string{"localhost:9092"},
        Topics:        []string{"test"},
        ConsumerGroup: "test-group",
        SASLMechanism: "aws-msk-iam",
        AssumeRoleARN: "arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/test-role",
    }
    fmt.Printf("Test 2 - AssumeRoleARN: '%s', CredentialsProfile: '%s'\n", cfg2.AssumeRoleARN, cfg2.CredentialsProfile)
    
    // Test 3: With CredentialsProfile
    cfg3 := configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig{
        Brokers:            []string{"localhost:9092"},
        Topics:             []string{"test"},
        ConsumerGroup:      "test-group",
        SASLMechanism:      "aws-msk-iam",
        CredentialsProfile: "test-profile",
    }
    fmt.Printf("Test 3 - AssumeRoleARN: '%s', CredentialsProfile: '%s'\n", cfg3.AssumeRoleARN, cfg3.CredentialsProfile)
    
    // Test 4: Both AssumeRoleARN and CredentialsProfile
    cfg4 := configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig{
        Brokers:            []string{"localhost:9092"},
        Topics:             []string{"test"},
        ConsumerGroup:      "test-group",
        SASLMechanism:      "aws-msk-iam",
        AssumeRoleARN:      "arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/test-role",
        CredentialsProfile: "test-profile",
    }
    fmt.Printf("Test 4 - AssumeRoleARN: '%s', CredentialsProfile: '%s'\n", cfg4.AssumeRoleARN, cfg4.CredentialsProfile)
    
    fmt.Println("All config tests passed!")
}
EOF

2026-05-29 21:36:47,601 - INFO - swea-agent-centrifugal__centrifugo-1129 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now let me think about edge cases more carefully and check if there's anything else I need to handle:

1. The `resolveAwsAuth` method correctly handles:
   - `AssumeRoleARN` set → use STS assume role (with optional `CredentialsProfile` for the source credentials)
   - `CredentialsProfile` set but no `AssumeRoleARN` → use profile credentials directly
   - Neither set → fall back to `SASLUser`/`SASLPassword` (backwards compatible)

2. The franz-go `aws.Auth` struct has a `SessionToken` field that I'm correctly populating from STS credentials.

3. The `ManagedStreamingIAM` function in franz-go allows passing a function that returns credentials. For a more robust implementation, we could use that to auto-refresh credentials. However, looking at the SQS consumer, it also just sets credentials once. And the original implementation also set credentials once. So this is consistent.

But wait, I should also consider: what if someone wants to use `CredentialsProfile` without `AssumeRoleARN`? In that case, we load the default AWS config with the specified profile, and then retrieve credentials from it. This matches the SQS consumer behavior where `CredentialsProfile` is used to select a profile.

Let me also verify that I haven't missed anything by looking at the SQS consumer implementation more carefully:

In `aws_sqs.go`:
- Line 58-60: If `CredentialsProfile` is set, it's added to `loadOpts`
- Line 85-90: If `AssumeRoleARN` is set, it creates an STS client and assume role provider, then sets it on `awsCfg.Credentials`

My implementation does the same but tailored for the franz-go auth struct. 

One more thing: I should consider adding a region configuration option. Looking at the SQS consumer, it has a `Region` field. The franz-go AWS SASL implementation auto-detects the region from the broker hostname. But the AWS config needs a region too. Let me check if `LoadDefaultConfig` requires a region...

Looking at the AWS SDK documentation, `LoadDefaultConfig` will look for region in environment variables, shared config, etc. If not found, it may fail. But the SQS consumer has a `Region` field that is required. For Kafka, the region is typically auto-detected from the broker hostname (the franz-go library does this). However, the STS client needs a region to call AssumeRole.

Actually, looking more carefully at the SQS consumer, the `Region` is used for both the SQS client and the STS client (via `awsCfg`). For Kafka, we should also have a way to specify the region. But the PR description doesn't mention it explicitly. Let me check if there's a way to handle this...

Actually, looking at the `aws_sqs.go` code again:
```go
loadOpts := []func(*config.LoadOptions) error{
    config.WithRegion(cfg.Region),
}
```

The region is always passed. But for Kafka, we don't have a region field. However, the `LoadDefaultConfig` will try to resolve the region from:
1. Environment variables
2. Shared config files
3. EC2 Instance Metadata Service (if running on EC2)
4. ECS task metadata (if running on ECS)

So if the user has AWS credentials configured properly (via env vars, instance profile, etc.), it should work. However, to be safe and consistent, we might want to add a `Region` field too. But the PR description doesn't mention it, and the SQS consumer already has it separately. Let me keep it as is for now - the region can be resolved automatically.

Actually, wait - I should also add a `Region` field to the Kafka config to make it more robust. Let me check the PR description again:

> Add `assume_role_arn` (or similar) and, optionally, `credentials_profile` options to the Kafka consumer config, matching the SQS consumer configuration style.

It mentions `assume_role_arn` and `credentials_profile`. It doesn't mention `region`. So I'll keep my implementation as is. The region will be auto-detected or can be set via standard AWS environment variables.

Now let me also think about whether I need to handle the case where `SASLMechanism` is `aws-msk-iam` and `AssumeRoleARN` is set, but `SASLUser`/`SASLPassword` are also set. My code correctly prioritizes `AssumeRoleARN`.

    24	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/kmsg"
    25	        franzaws "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/aws"
    26	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/plain"
    27	        "github.com/twmb/franz-go/pkg/sasl/scram"
    28	)
    29	
    30	type KafkaConfig = configtypes.KafkaConsumerConfig
    31	
    32	type testOnlyConfig struct {
    33	        topicPartitionBeforePauseCh    chan topicPartition
    34	        topicPartitionPauseProceedCh   chan struct{}
    35	        fetchTopicPartitionSubmittedCh chan kgo.FetchTopicPartition
    36	        // leaveGroupOnShutdown when true sends a manual LeaveGroup request on shutdown.
    37	        // Used only in tests to simulate the old behavior with dynamic instance IDs.
    38	        leaveGroupOnShutdown bool
    39	}
    40	
    41	type topicPartition struct {
    42	        topic     string
    43	        partition int32
    44	}
    45	
    46	type KafkaConsumer struct {
    47	        name       string
    48	        client     *kgo.Client
    49	        dispatcher Dispatcher
    50	        config     KafkaConfig
    51	        // consumersMu protects the consumers map. During normal operation, concurrent access is already
    52	        // prevented: franz-go serializes assigned/revoked/lost callbacks, and BlockRebalanceOnPoll
    53	        // prevents callbacks from firing while pollUntilFatal reads the map. The mutex is needed
    54	        // for shutdown, when the main goroutine accesses the map (via allTopicPartitions/killConsumers)
    55	        // while the group management goroutine may still be running callbacks. Must not be held
    56	        // during killConsumers' wg.Wait() to avoid blocking concurrent callbacks.
    57	        consumersMu    sync.Mutex
    58	        consumers      map[topicPartition]*partitionConsumer
    59	        doneCh         chan struct{}
    60	        common         *consumerCommon
    61	        testOnlyConfig testOnlyConfig
    62	}
    63	
    64	func NewKafkaConsumer(
    65	        config KafkaConfig, dispatcher Dispatcher, common *consumerCommon,
    66	) (*KafkaConsumer, error) {
    67	        if len(config.Brokers) == 0 {
    68	                return nil, errors.New("brokers required")
    69	        }
    70	        if len(config.Topics) == 0 {
    71	                return nil, errors.New("topics required")
    72	        }
    73	        if len(config.ConsumerGroup) == 0 {
    74	                return nil, errors.New("consumer_group required")
    75	        }
    76	        if config.MaxPollRecords == 0 {
    77	                config.MaxPollRecords = 100
    78	        }
    79	        if config.FetchMaxWait == 0 {
    80	                config.FetchMaxWait = configtypes.Duration(500 * time.Millisecond)
    81	        }
    82	        if config.PartitionQueueMaxSize == -1 {
    83	                config.PartitionQueueMaxSize = 0
    84	        } else if config.PartitionQueueMaxSize == 0 {
    85	                config.PartitionQueueMaxSize = 1000
    86	        }
    87	        consumer := &KafkaConsumer{
    88	                name:       common.name,
    89	                dispatcher: dispatcher,
    90	                config:     config,
    91	                consumers:  make(map[topicPartition]*partitionConsumer),
    92	                doneCh:     make(chan struct{}),
    93	                common:     common,
    94	        }
    95	        cl, err := consumer.initClient()
    96	        if err != nil {
    97	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error init Kafka client: %w", err)
    98	        }
    99	        consumer.client = cl
   100	        return consumer, nil
   101	}
   102	
   103	const kafkaClientID = "centrifugo"
   104	
   105	// kgoZerologAdapter adapts zerolog.Logger to kgo.Logger interface.
   106	type kgoZerologAdapter struct {
   107	        log zerolog.Logger
   108	}
   109	
   110	func (l *kgoZerologAdapter) Level() kgo.LogLevel {
   111	        switch {
   112	        case logging.Enabled(logging.TraceLevel):
   113	                return kgo.LogLevelDebug // Kafka client debug level is more like our trace.
   114	        case logging.Enabled(logging.InfoLevel):
   115	                return kgo.LogLevelInfo
   116	        case logging.Enabled(logging.WarnLevel):
   117	                return kgo.LogLevelWarn
   118	        case logging.Enabled(logging.ErrorLevel):
   119	                return kgo.LogLevelError
   120	        default:
   121	                return kgo.LogLevelNone
   122	        }
   123	}
   124	
   125	func (l *kgoZerologAdapter) Log(level kgo.LogLevel, msg string, keyvals ...any) {
   126	        var e *zerolog.Event
   127	        switch level {
   128	        case kgo.LogLevelError:
   129	                e = l.log.Error()
   130	        case kgo.LogLevelWarn:
   131	                e = l.log.Warn()
   132	        case kgo.LogLevelInfo:
   133	                e = l.log.Info()
   134	        case kgo.LogLevelDebug:
   135	                e = l.log.Trace() // Kafka client debug level is more like our trace.
   136	        default:
   137	                return
   138	        }
   139	        for i := 0; i+1 < len(keyvals); i += 2 {
   140	                if key, ok := keyvals[i].(string); ok {
   141	                        e = e.Interface(key, keyvals[i+1])
   142	                }
   143	        }
   144	        e.Msg(msg)
   145	}
   146	
   147	// leaveGroup sends a manual LeaveGroup request for static members. franz-go's Close does not
   148	// send LeaveGroup for static members by design. This method is only used in tests to simulate
   149	// the old behavior where dynamic instance IDs were combined with a manual LeaveGroup.
   150	func (c *KafkaConsumer) leaveGroup(ctx context.Context, client *kgo.Client) error {
   151	        req := kmsg.NewPtrLeaveGroupRequest()
   152	        req.Group = c.config.ConsumerGroup
   153	        instanceID := c.config.InstanceID
   154	        reason := "shutdown"
   155	        req.Members = []kmsg.LeaveGroupRequestMember{
   156	                {
   157	                        InstanceID: &instanceID,
   158	                        Reason:     &reason,
   159	                },
   160	        }
   161	        _, err := req.RequestWith(ctx, client)
   162	        return err
   163	}
   164	
   165	func (c *KafkaConsumer) initClient() (*kgo.Client, error) {
   166	        opts := []kgo.Opt{
   167	                kgo.SeedBrokers(c.config.Brokers...),
   168	                kgo.ConsumeTopics(c.config.Topics...),
   169	                kgo.ConsumerGroup(c.config.ConsumerGroup),
   170	                kgo.OnPartitionsAssigned(c.assigned),
   171	                kgo.OnPartitionsRevoked(c.revoked),
   172	                kgo.OnPartitionsLost(c.lost),
   173	                kgo.AutoCommitMarks(),
   174	                kgo.BlockRebalanceOnPoll(),
   175	                kgo.ClientID(kafkaClientID),
   176	                kgo.FetchMaxWait(time.Duration(c.config.FetchMaxWait)),
   177	                kgo.WithLogger(&kgoZerologAdapter{log: c.common.log}),
   178	        }
   179	        if c.config.InstanceID != "" { // Important to keep separate, otherwise pointer to empty string is used in kgo.InstanceID.
   180	                opts = append(opts, kgo.InstanceID(c.config.InstanceID))
   181	        }
   182	        if c.config.FetchMaxBytes > 0 {
   183	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchMaxBytes(c.config.FetchMaxBytes))
   184	        }
   185	        if !c.config.FetchReadUncommitted {
   186	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadCommitted()))
   187	        } else {
   188	                opts = append(opts, kgo.FetchIsolationLevel(kgo.ReadUncommitted()))
   189	        }
   190	        if c.config.TLS.Enabled {
   191	                tlsConfig, err := c.config.TLS.ToGoTLSConfig("kafka:" + c.name)
   192	                if err != nil {
   193	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("error making TLS configuration: %w", err)
   194	                }
   195	                dialer := &tls.Dialer{
   196	                        NetDialer: &net.Dialer{Timeout: 10 * time.Second},
   197	                        Config:    tlsConfig,
   198	                }
   199	                opts = append(opts, kgo.Dialer(dialer.DialContext))
   200	        }
   201	
   202	        if c.config.SASLMechanism != "" {
   203	                switch c.config.SASLMechanism {
   204	                case "plain":
   205	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(plain.Auth{
   206	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   207	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   208	                        }.AsMechanism()))
   209	                case "scram-sha-256":
   210	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   211	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   212	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   213	                        }.AsSha256Mechanism()))
   214	                case "scram-sha-512":
   215	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(scram.Auth{
   216	                                User: c.config.SASLUser,
   217	                                Pass: c.config.SASLPassword,
   218	                        }.AsSha512Mechanism()))
   219	                case "aws-msk-iam":
   220	                        awsAuth, err := c.resolveAwsAuth()
   221	                        if err != nil {
   222	                                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error resolving AWS auth: %w", err)
   223	                        }
   224	                        opts = append(opts, kgo.SASL(awsAuth.AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()))
   225	                default:
   226	                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported SASL mechanism: %s", c.config.SASLMechanism)
   227	                }
   228	        }
   229	
   230	        client, err := kgo.NewClient(opts...)
   231	        if err != nil {
   232	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error initializing client: %w", err)
   233	        }
   234	        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   235	        defer cancel()
   236	        if err := client.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
   237	                client.Close()
   238	                return nil, fmt.Errorf("error ping Kafka: %w", err)
   239	        }
   240	        return client, nil
   241	}
   242	
   243	// resolveAwsAuth resolves AWS credentials for MSK IAM authentication.
   244	// If AssumeRoleARN is provided, it uses STS to assume the role and obtain temporary credentials.
   245	// If CredentialsProfile is provided, it uses the specified shared credentials profile.
   246	// Otherwise, it falls back to the SASL user/password fields for backwards compatibility.
   247	func (c *KafkaConsumer) resolveAwsAuth() (franzaws.Auth, error) {
   248	        // If AssumeRoleARN is provided, use STS AssumeRole to get temporary credentials.
   249	        if c.config.AssumeRoleARN != "" {
   250	                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   251	                defer cancel()
   252	
   253	                loadOpts := []func(*awsconfig.LoadOptions) error{}
   254	                if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
   255	                        loadOpts = append(loadOpts, awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile))
   256	                }
   257	
   258	                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx, loadOpts...)
   259	                if err != nil {
   260	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config: %w", err)
   261	                }
   262	
   263	                stsClient := sts.NewFromConfig(awsCfg)
   264	                assumeRoleProvider := stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider(stsClient, c.config.AssumeRoleARN)
   265	
   266	                creds, err := assumeRoleProvider.Retrieve(ctx)
   267	                if err != nil {
   268	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to assume role: %w", err)
   269	                }
   270	
   271	                return franzaws.Auth{
   272	                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
   273	                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
   274	                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
   275	                }, nil
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        // If CredentialsProfile is provided (but no AssumeRoleARN), load credentials from the profile.
   279	        if c.config.CredentialsProfile != "" {
   280	                ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   281	                defer cancel()
   282	
   283	                awsCfg, err := awsconfig.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx,
   284	                        awsconfig.WithSharedConfigProfile(c.config.CredentialsProfile),
   285	                )
   286	                if err != nil {
   287	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to load AWS config with profile: %w", err)
   288	                }
   289	
   290	                creds, err := awsCfg.Credentials.Retrieve(ctx)
   291	                if err != nil {
   292	                        return franzaws.Auth{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to retrieve credentials from profile: %w", err)
   293	                }
   294	
   295	                return franzaws.Auth{
   296	                        AccessKey:    creds.AccessKeyID,
   297	                        SecretKey:    creds.SecretAccessKey,
   298	                        SessionToken: creds.SessionToken,
   299	                }, nil
   300	        }
   301	
   302	        // Fall back to SASL user/password for backwards compatibility.
   303	        return franzaws.Auth{
   304	                AccessKey: c.config.SASLUser,
   305	                SecretKey: c.config.SASLPassword,
   306	        }, nil
   307	}
   308	
   309	func (c *KafkaConsumer) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   310	        defer func() {

Now let me also check if there's any additional config validation needed. Looking at the SQS consumer, the `Validate` method doesn't validate `AssumeRoleARN` or `CredentialsProfile` - they're optional. Same for Kafka.

Let me also check if there's a `Region` field we might need. Looking at the SQS consumer config:

```go
Region string `mapstructure:"region" ...`
```

But for Kafka, the region is auto-detected from the broker hostname by the franz-go library. The STS client we create will use the region from the AWS config (which is loaded from environment, shared config, or instance metadata). So we should be fine.

However, I realize there's a potential issue: if someone uses `AssumeRoleARN` without having AWS credentials configured in the environment (e.g., they want to use the `SASLUser`/`SASLPassword` as the base credentials for assuming the role), my implementation wouldn't support that. But that's an edge case that's unlikely - typically if you're using STS, you have some form of AWS credentials available (env vars, instance profile, etc.). And the PR description says this should match the SQS consumer configuration style, which also doesn't support using the direct keys for the base credentials.

Actually, wait - looking at the PR description again:
> The configuration should continue to support direct key input for backwards compatibility, but if both are set, `assume_role_arn` should take precedence.

This implies that `assume_role_arn` should work with the direct keys as the base credentials. But that's not how the SQS consumer works either - the SQS consumer uses the default credential chain (env, profile, etc.) for the base credentials. So my implementation is consistent with SQS.

Let me run one more check to make sure everything compiles correctly with the original go.mod:

Everything compiles and vets cleanly. Let me now think about whether I should also add a `Region` field to the Kafka config. Looking at this more carefully:

The STS `AssumeRole` API requires a regional endpoint. If the user doesn't have a region configured via environment variables or shared config, the `LoadDefaultConfig` call might fail. The SQS consumer has an explicit `Region` field, which makes sense because SQS is regional. For Kafka, the brokers might be in any region, and the franz-go library auto-detects the region from the broker hostname for the SASL handshake.

But for the STS call, we need a region. If the user has AWS config set up properly (e.g., via `AWS_REGION` env var or `~/.aws/config`), this should work. If not, they might get an error. However, I think this is acceptable for now - the PR description doesn't mention a `region` field, and users who are using STS are likely to have their AWS environment configured properly.

Let me make sure my changes are complete. Let me review the final diff

Everything looks good. Let me now think about any remaining edge cases:

1. **Empty AssumeRoleARN with CredentialsProfile**: This works - we load credentials from the specified profile.

2. **Empty CredentialsProfile with AssumeRoleARN**: This works - we load default AWS config and assume the role.

3. **Both empty**: Falls back to SASLUser/SASLPassword.

4. **Both set**: AssumeRoleARN takes precedence, and CredentialsProfile is used as the source profile for the base credentials when assuming the role.

5. **Error handling**: All AWS calls have proper error wrapping.

One thing I want to double-check: in the `resolveAwsAuth` function, when `AssumeRoleARN` is set and we also have `CredentialsProfile`, we add the profile to `loadOpts`. But `LoadDefaultConfig` also reads from environment variables. If the user has both a profile and env vars set, the behavior follows the standard AWS SDK precedence. This is consistent with the SQS consumer.

I believe my implementation is complete and correct. Let me clean up the temporary go1.26.0 download and submit:

Actually, let me keep the go1.26.0 - it was downloaded by the test infrastructure. Let me just make sure everything is in order.

-	// DisableClosingHandshake disables WebSocket closing handshake. This restores the behavior prior to
-	// Centrifugo v6.5.1 where server never sent a close frame on connection close initiated by server.
-	// Normally closing handshake is recommended to be performed according to WebSocket protocol RFC,
-	// so this option is useful only in some specific cases when you need to restore the previous behavior.
-	DisableClosingHandshake bool `mapstructure:"disable_closing_handshake" json:"disable_closing_handshake" envconfig:"disable_closing_handshake" yaml:"disable_closing_handshake" toml:"disable_closing_handshake"`
-	// DisableDisconnectPush disables sending disconnect push messages to clients. It's sent by default to make
-	// unidirectional transports similar, but since uni_websocket transport also sends close frame to the client
-	// with the same code/reason – some users may want to disable disconnect push to avoid ambiguity.
-	DisableDisconnectPush bool `mapstructure:"disable_disconnect_push" json:"disable_disconnect_push" envconfig:"disable_disconnect_push" yaml:"disable_disconnect_push" toml:"disable_disconnect_push"`
-	// JoinPushMessages when enabled allow uni_websocket transport to join messages together into
-	// one frame using Centrifugal client protocol delimiters: new line for JSON protocol and
-	// length-prefixed format for Protobuf protocol. This can be useful to reduce system call
-	// overhead when sending many small messages. The client side must be ready to handle such
-	// joined messages coming in one WebSocket frame.
-	JoinPushMessages bool `mapstructure:"join_push_messages" json:"join_push_messages" envconfig:"join_push_messages" yaml:"join_push_messages" toml:"join_push_messages"`

+        CompressionLevel   int      `mapstructure:"compression_level" json:"compression_level" envconfig:"compression_level" default:"1" yaml:"compression_level" toml:"compression_level"`
+        ReadBufferSize     int      `mapstructure:"read_buffer_size" json:"read_buffer_size" envconfig:"read_buffer_size" yaml:"read_buffer_size" toml:"read_buffer_size"`

+        MessageSizeLimit   int      `mapstructure:"message_size_limit" json:"message_size_limit" envconfig:"message_size_limit" default:"65536" yaml:"message_size_limit" toml:"message_size_limit"`
+        // DisableClosingHandshake disables WebSocket closing handshake. This restores the behavior prior to
+        // Centrifugo v6.5.1 where server never sent a close frame on connection close initiated by server.
+        // Normally closing handshake is recommended to be performed according to WebSocket protocol RFC,
+        // so this option is useful only in some specific cases when you need to restore the previous behavior.
+        DisableClosingHandshake bool `mapstructure:"disable_closing_handshake" json:"disable_closing_handshake" envconfig:"disable_closing_handshake" yaml:"disable_closing_handshake" toml:"disable_closing_handshake"`
+        // DisableDisconnectPush disables sending disconnect push messages to clients. It's sent by default to make
+        // unidirectional transports similar, but since uni_websocket transport also sends close frame to the client
+        // with the same code/reason – some users may want to disable disconnect push to avoid ambiguity.
+        DisableDisconnectPush bool `mapstructure:"disable_disconnect_push" json:"disable_disconnect_push" envconfig:"disable_disconnect_push" yaml:"disable_disconnect_push" toml:"disable_disconnect_push"`
+        // JoinPushMessages when enabled allow uni_websocket transport to join messages together into
+        // one frame using Centrifugal client protocol delimiters: new line for JSON protocol and
+        // length-prefixed format for Protobuf protocol. This can be useful to reduce system call
+        // overhead when sending many small messages. The client side must be ready to handle such
+        // joined messages coming in one WebSocket frame.
+        JoinPushMessages bool `mapstructure:"join_push_messages" json:"join_push_messages" envconfig:"join_push_messages" yaml:"join_push_messages" toml:"join_push_messages"`
 }

-		return fmt.Errorf("error parsing items from JSON: %v", err)
-	}
-	*d = items
-	return nil
+        // If the source is a string and the target is a slice, try to parse it as JSON.
+        var items ConnectCodeToHTTPResponseTransforms
+        err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &items)
+        if err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("error parsing items from JSON: %v", err)
+        }
+        *d = items
+        return nil
 }

 // PingPong allows configuring application level ping-pong behavior.
 // Note that in current implementation PingPongConfig.PingInterval must be greater than PingPongConfig.PongTimeout.
 type PingPong struct {
-	// PingInterval tells how often to issue server-to-client pings.
-	// To disable sending app-level pings use -1.
-	PingInterval Duration `mapstructure:"ping_interval" json:"ping_interval" envconfig:"ping_interval" default:"25s" yaml:"ping_interval" toml:"ping_interval"`
-	// PongTimeout sets time for pong check after issuing a ping. To disable pong checks use -1.
-	// PongTimeout must be less than PingInterval in current implementation.
-	PongTimeout Duration `mapstructure:"pong_timeout" json:"pong_timeout" envconfig:"pong_timeout" default:"8s" yaml:"pong_timeout" toml:"pong_timeout"`
+        // PingInterval tells how often to issue server-to-client pings.
+        // To disable sending app-level pings use -1.
+        PingInterval Duration `mapstructure:"ping_interval" json:"ping_interval" envconfig:"ping_interval" default:"25s" yaml:"ping_interval" toml:"ping_interval"`
+        // PongTimeout sets time for pong check after issuing a ping. To disable pong checks use -1.
+        // PongTimeout must be less than PingInterval in current implementation.
+        PongTimeout Duration `mapstructure:"pong_timeout" json:"pong_timeout" envconfig:"pong_timeout" default:"8s" yaml:"pong_timeout" toml:"pong_timeout"`
 }

 // NatsBroker configuration.
 type NatsBroker struct {
-	NatsPrefixed `mapstructure:",squash" yaml:",inline"`
-
-	// AllowWildcards allows to enable wildcard subscriptions. By default, wildcard subscriptions
-	// are not allowed. Using wildcard subscriptions can't be combined with join/leave events and presence
-	// because subscriptions do not belong to a concrete channel after with wildcards, while join/leave events
-	// require concrete channel to be published. And presence does not make a lot of sense for wildcard
-	// subscriptions - there could be subscribers which use different mask, but still receive subset of updates.
-	// It's required to use channels without wildcards to for mentioned features to work properly. When
-	// using wildcard subscriptions a special care is needed regarding security - pay additional
-	// attention to a proper permission management.
-	AllowWildcards bool `mapstructure:"allow_wildcards" json:"allow_wildcards" envconfig:"allow_wildcards" yaml:"allow_wildcards" toml:"allow_wildcards"`
-
-	// RawMode allows enabling raw communication with Nats. When on, Centrifugo subscribes to channels
-	// without adding any prefixes to channel name. Proper prefixes must be managed by the application in this
-	// case. Data consumed from Nats is sent directly to subscribers without any processing. When publishing
-	// to Nats Centrifugo does not add any prefixes to channel names also. Centrifugo features like Publication
-	// tags, Publication ClientInfo, join/leave events are not supported in raw mode.
-	RawMode RawModeConfig `mapstructure:"raw_mode" json:"raw_mode" envconfig:"raw_mode" yaml:"raw_mode" toml:"raw_mode"`
+        NatsPrefixed `mapstructure:",squash" yaml:",inline"`
+
+        // AllowWildcards allows to enable wildcard subscriptions. By default, wildcard subscriptions
+        // are not allowed. Using wildcard subscriptions can't be combined with join/leave events and presence
+        // because subscriptions do not belong to a concrete channel after with wildcards, while join/leave events
+        // require concrete channel to be published. And presence does not make a lot of sense for wildcard
+        // subscriptions - there could be subscribers which use different mask, but still receive subset of updates.
+        // It's required to use channels without wildcards to for mentioned features to work properly. When
+        // using wildcard subscriptions a special care is needed regarding security - pay additional
+        // attention to a proper permission management.
+        AllowWildcards bool `mapstructure:"allow_wildcards" json:"allow_wildcards" envconfig:"allow_wildcards" yaml:"allow_wildcards" toml:"allow_wildcards"`
+
+        // RawMode allows enabling raw communication with Nats. When on, Centrifugo subscribes to channels
+        // without adding any prefixes to channel name. Proper prefixes must be managed by the application in this
+        // case. Data consumed from Nats is sent directly to subscribers without any processing. When publishing
+        // to Nats Centrifugo does not add any prefixes to channel names also. Centrifugo features like Publication
+        // tags, Publication ClientInfo, join/leave events are not supported in raw mode.
+        RawMode RawModeConfig `mapstructure:"raw_mode" json:"raw_mode" envconfig:"raw_mode" yaml:"raw_mode" toml:"raw_mode"`
 }
 
 type RawModeConfig struct {
-	// Enabled enables raw mode when true.
-	Enabled bool `mapstructure:"enabled" json:"enabled" envconfig:"enabled" yaml:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
+        // Enabled enables raw mode when true.
+        Enabled bool `mapstructure:"enabled" json:"enabled" envconfig:"enabled" yaml:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
 
-	// ChannelReplacements is a map where keys are strings to replace and values are replacements.
-	// For example, you have Centrifugo namespace "chat" and using channel "chat:index", but you want to
-	// use channel "chat.index" in Nats. Then you can define SymbolReplacements map like this: `{":": "."}`.
-	// In this case Centrifugo will replace all ":" symbols in channel name with "." before sending to Nats.
-	// Broker keeps reverse mapping to the original channel to broadcast to proper channels when processing
-	// messages received from Nats.
-	ChannelReplacements MapStringString `mapstructure:"channel_replacements" default:"{}" json:"channel_replacements" envconfig:"channel_replacements" yaml:"channel_replacements" toml:"channel_replacements"`
+        // ChannelReplacements is a map where keys are strings to replace and values are replacements.
+        // For example, you have Centrifugo namespace "chat" and using channel "chat:index", but you want to
+        // use channel "chat.index" in Nats. Then you can define SymbolReplacements map like this: `{":": "."}`.
+        // In this case Centrifugo will replace all ":" symbols in channel name with "." before sending to Nats.
+        // Broker keeps reverse mapping to the original channel to broadcast to proper channels when processing
+        // messages received from Nats.
+        ChannelReplacements MapStringString `mapstructure:"channel_replacements" default:"{}" json:"channel_replacements" envconfig:"channel_replacements" yaml:"channel_replacements" toml:"channel_replacements"`
 
-	// Prefix is a string that will be added to all channels when publishing messages to Nats, subscribing
-	// to channels in Nats. It's also stripped from channel name when processing messages received from Nats.
-	// By default, no prefix is used.
-	Prefix string `mapstructure:"prefix" json:"prefix" envconfig:"prefix" yaml:"prefix" toml:"prefix"`
+        // Prefix is a string that will be added to all channels when publishing messages to Nats, subscribing
+        // to channels in Nats. It's also stripped from channel name when processing messages received from Nats.
+        // By default, no prefix is used.
+        Prefix string `mapstructure:"prefix" json:"prefix" envconfig:"prefix" yaml:"prefix" toml:"prefix"`
 }

-	Proxy   `mapstructure:",squash" yaml:",inline"`
+        // Enabled must be true to tell Centrifugo to enable the configured proxy.
+        Enabled bool `mapstructure:"enabled" json:"enabled" envconfig:"enabled" yaml:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
+        Proxy   `mapstructure:",squash" yaml:",inline"`
 }
